  vehicle_assigned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a company vehicle has been assigned to this sales representative. True for DSD drivers and field sales reps with company cars; false for inside sales and office-based roles.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person or system that created this sales representative record. Used for audit trail and data governance.']

  Note: 'Sales representative master record for field sales, key account management, and inside sales personnel operating within the sales domain. Captures employee ID reference, rep name, rep type (field sales/key account manager/national account manager/inside sales/DSD driver), assigned territory, channel specialization, hire date, active status, SFA user ID, and current quota period. Distinct from the workforce domain employee record — this is the sales-domain operational view of the selling resource.'
}

Table sales.quota {
  quota_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sales quota assignment record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: In consumer goods, incremental sales quotas are set in the context of specific marketing campaigns (e.g., new product launch campaigns). Linking quota to campaign enables campaign ROI measurement via ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Quota attainment drives incentive compensation expense charged to cost centers. Consumer goods companies allocate sales incentive costs to cost centers for SG&A budget control and compensation expense']
  finance_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.finance_budget. Business justification: Sales quotas are derived from and aligned to financial budgets in consumer goods IBP/S&OP planning cycles. Linking quota to finance_budget enables variance analysis between commercial targets and fina']
  hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product category to which this quota applies.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the brand to which this quota applies.']
  sales_rep_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales representative to whom this quota is assigned.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: New product launch quotas and SKU-level sales targets are standard in consumer goods: reps receive specific volume quotas per SKU for new item sell-in. quota already links to hierarchy for category-le']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales territory to which this quota is assigned.']
  accelerator_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Enhanced commission rate applied when stretch target is exceeded.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Methodology used to allocate quota across territories or reps (e.g., historical performance, market potential, equal distribution).']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for the quota assignment.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the manager or executive who approved the quota.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which the quota was approved.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Sales channel to which this quota applies. DSD = Direct Store Delivery, DTC = Direct to Consumer.']
  commission_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Commission percentage rate applied when quota is achieved.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the quota record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary quota values.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment or account classification to which this quota applies (e.g., national accounts, regional chains, independent retailers).']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Distribution channel code for this quota assignment.']
  division varchar [note: 'Business division or product line code for this quota assignment.']
  fiscal_month varchar [note: 'Fiscal month within the year (1-12) for which the quota is assigned.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter within the year (1-4) for which the quota is assigned.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year for which the quota is assigned.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market to which this quota applies (e.g., Northeast Region, California, National).']
  minimum_threshold decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum performance threshold required for quota credit or commission eligibility.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that last modified the quota record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the quota record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the quota assignment.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the quota period.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the quota period.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Time period granularity for the quota (monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, or annual).']
  product_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of products covered by this quota (all products, specific category, specific brand, or specific SKU).']
  quota_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name for the quota assignment.']
  quota_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier or reference number for the quota assignment.']
  quota_source varchar [note: 'Method by which the quota was determined. Top-down = allocated from corporate targets, Bottom-up = aggregated from field input, Negotiated = agreed between management and rep, Algorithmic = calculated by predictive model.']
  quota_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the quota assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|active|suspended|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  quota_type varchar [note: 'Type of quota being assigned. Revenue = monetary sales target, Volume = unit/case target, New Distribution = new store/outlet target, TDP (Total Distribution Points) = weighted distribution target, ACV (All Commodity Volume) = market coverage target, Unit Sales = individual unit target, Gross Margin = profitability target. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: revenue|volume|new_distribution|tdp|acv|unit_sales|gross_margin — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  quota_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Target value for the quota. Interpretation depends on quota_type: monetary amount for revenue quotas, unit count for volume quotas, store count for distribution quotas.']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'Sales organization code responsible for this quota assignment.']
  stretch_target decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aspirational or stretch goal beyond the base quota value, used for incentive compensation.']
  uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quota value (e.g., USD, cases, units, stores, points).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created the quota record.']

  Note: 'Sales quota assignment record defining revenue, volume, and distribution targets for sales representatives, territories, or account teams for a given planning period. Captures quota period (month/quarter/year), quota type (revenue/volume/new distribution/TDP), assigned rep or territory, product category or brand, quota value, stretch target, currency, approval status, and quota source (top-down/bottom-up/negotiated). Enables field sales KPI attainment tracking and S&OP alignment.'
}

Table sales.distribution_point {
  distribution_point_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the distribution point record. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: TDP (Total Distribution Points) gains in consumer goods are frequently activated as part of new product launch or promotional campaigns. Linking distribution_point to campaign enables measurement of d']
  consumer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.consumer_segment. Business justification: Distribution points in consumer goods target specific consumer segments (e.g., premium SKUs distributed only in stores serving premium segments). Consumer segment linkage drives distribution strategy,']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_facility. Business justification: TDP (Total Distribution Points) reporting: distribution_point tracks SKU availability by account/channel and must reference the DC physically handling that product. distribution_source is a plain-text']
  label_version_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.label_version. Business justification: Shelf compliance process: each distribution_point must carry the jurisdiction-approved label version for that market. Consumer goods field teams and trade compliance teams verify that the label versio']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Distribution points are managed per brand to ensure correct shelf placement and brand‑level inventory control.']
  registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_registration. Business justification: Market activation process: before a distribution_point is activated in a new market, sales operations must verify the SKU holds a valid regulatory_registration in that jurisdiction. Consumer goods com']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific retail store location where the SKU is available for sale.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Distribution points record which SKU is allocated to each retail location; required for inventory allocation and shelf‑stocking dashboards.']
  supplier_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_site. Business justification: VMI programs tie a store distribution point to the supplier site that replenishes it, enabling inventory‑level monitoring.']
  supply_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.network_node. Business justification: Distribution Point Inventory Planning uses the supplying network node to compute safety stock, replenishment, and service level targets.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail trade account or customer where the SKU is distributed.']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the SKU became actively distributed and available for sale at this point.']
  actual_retail_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current actual retail selling price observed at this distribution point, may differ from RSP/MSRP.']
  acv_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The ACV percentage contribution of this retail location to the total category volume, used for weighted distribution metrics.']
  acv_weighted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution point is included in ACV-weighted distribution calculations (True/False). ACV-weighted stores represent a significant portion of category sales volume.']
  authorization_date date [note: 'Date when the SKU was authorized for distribution at this retail location or account.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Retail channel classification for this distribution point (grocery, mass merchandiser, drug, convenience, club, e-commerce, specialty). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: grocery|mass_merchandiser|drug|convenience|club|ecommerce|specialty — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this distribution point record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for pricing at this distribution point.']
  delist_date date [note: 'Date when the SKU was delisted or removed from distribution at this location. Null if still active.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'SAP SD distribution channel code representing the route-to-market for this distribution point.']
  distribution_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SKU at this distribution point. Active indicates the product is currently distributed and available for sale; authorized indicates approval but not yet active; delisted indicates removal from distribution; inactive indicates temporary suspension.']
  distribution_verification_date date [note: 'Date when the distribution status was last verified or confirmed through the distribution source.']
  division varchar [note: 'SAP division code representing the product line or business unit for this distribution.']
  dsd_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution point is serviced via Direct Store Delivery (True) or warehouse distribution (False).']
  edi_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution point supports EDI transactions for order and inventory management (True/False).']
  facing_count varchar [note: 'Number of product facings (front-facing units) allocated to this SKU on the retail shelf at this location.']
  geographic_market varchar [note: 'Geographic market or region code for this distribution point, used for regional distribution analysis.']
  last_pos_sale_date date [note: 'Most recent date when a POS transaction was recorded for this SKU at this location, used to verify active distribution.']
  launch_wave varchar [note: 'Launch wave or phase identifier for new product rollouts, indicating the sequence of distribution expansion.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified this distribution point record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this distribution point record was last modified or updated.']
  msrp_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Manufacturer suggested retail price for this SKU at this distribution point.']
  new_product_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution point represents a new product introduction (True/False). Used for NPD distribution tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding special conditions, exceptions, or context for this distribution point.']
  osa_status varchar [note: 'Current on-shelf availability status of the SKU at this location, indicating whether the product is physically available for purchase.']
  planogram_slot_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the planogram slot or shelf position where this SKU is merchandised at the store.']
  pog_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU placement at this location is compliant with the authorized planogram (True/False).']
  rsp_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Manufacturer recommended selling price for this SKU at this distribution point.']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'SAP sales organization code responsible for this distribution point.']
  shelf_position varchar [note: 'Vertical and horizontal shelf position classification for the SKU at this location (eye level, top shelf, middle shelf, bottom shelf, end cap, promotional display).']
  tdp_count_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution point is counted in TDP metrics (True/False). Used to calculate numeric distribution reach.']
  vmi_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this distribution point operates under a Vendor Managed Inventory agreement (True/False).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of the person or process that created this distribution point record.']

  Note: 'Total Distribution Points (TDP) record tracking which SKUs are actively distributed and available for sale at specific retail accounts or store locations. Captures store/account reference, SKU/GTIN, distribution status (active/inactive/authorized/delisted), authorization date, delist date, channel, planogram slot reference, ACV-weighted distribution flag, and distribution source (sell-in confirmed/POS verified). Core entity for ACV/TDP metric computation and new product distribution tracking.'
}

Table sales.price_list {
  price_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the price list record. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Promotional price lists in consumer goods are created specifically for marketing campaigns (e.g., campaign-period pricing for a product launch). Linking price_list to campaign enables campaign cost tr']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Price lists are often brand‑specific, enabling brand‑level pricing strategy and compliance.']
  superseded_by_price_list_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer price list that replaces this one when status is superseded. Enables price list succession tracking and historical pricing analysis.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status for the price list. Tracks progression through pricing governance approval process before activation.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the pricing manager or executive who approved this price list for activation. Required for audit trail and pricing governance compliance.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the price list was approved for activation. Critical for audit trail and compliance with pricing governance policies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this price list record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which all prices in this list are denominated. Critical for multi-currency pricing operations and foreign exchange management.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Distribution channel to which this price list applies. Enables channel-specific pricing strategies for retail stores, wholesale distributors, direct-to-consumer (DTC), e-commerce platforms, foodservice operators, and institutional buyers.']
  division varchar [note: 'SAP SD division code representing the product line or business unit to which this price list applies. Enables division-specific pricing strategies across product portfolios.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the price list expires and is no longer valid for new pricing calculations. Nullable for open-ended price lists. Aligns with SAP SD condition record validity period.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the price list becomes valid and active for pricing calculations in sales orders and quotations. Aligns with SAP SD condition record validity period.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'External system identifier or reference number for this price list in the source system. Enables cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracking.']
  freight_inclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether freight costs are included in the prices in this list. True when prices are freight-inclusive (delivered pricing), false when prices are ex-works or FOB.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic region, country, or territory to which this price list applies. Supports region-specific pricing strategies and regulatory compliance with local pricing regulations.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining delivery and risk transfer terms associated with prices in this list (e.g., FOB, CIF, DDP). Clarifies freight and insurance responsibilities.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum order quantity required for prices in this list to apply. Used to enforce volume thresholds and support tiered pricing strategies.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this price list record. Required for audit trail and accountability in pricing governance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this price list record was last modified. Supports change tracking and audit trail for pricing governance compliance.']
  moq_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the minimum order quantity threshold. May differ from price UoM to support different ordering and pricing units.']
  msrp_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price list contains manufacturer suggested retail prices (MSRP). True when prices represent official MSRP for consumer-facing pricing and promotional compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes and comments about the price list including special conditions, exceptions, or business context for pricing decisions.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms associated with this price list (e.g., Net 30, 2/10 Net 30). Defines credit period and early payment discount eligibility for customers purchasing under this list.']
  price_basis varchar [note: 'Pricing methodology or basis used to establish prices in this list. List price indicates standard published pricing, cost plus indicates markup over cost, market based indicates alignment with market rates, competitive indicates positioning relative to competitors, value based indicates pricing tied to customer value perception.']
  price_list_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the price list used in SAP SD pricing procedures and condition records. Externally-known identifier used in pricing agreements and sales documents.']
  price_list_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the price list purpose, scope, and applicability including target customer segments and product categories covered.']
  price_list_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive business name of the price list for identification and reporting purposes.']
  price_list_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price list. Draft indicates under development, pending approval indicates awaiting authorization, active indicates currently in use, inactive indicates temporarily disabled, expired indicates past validity period, superseded indicates replaced by newer version.']
  price_list_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price list by its business purpose and application context. Standard lists provide baseline pricing, promotional lists support time-limited campaigns, contract lists support negotiated agreements, channel-specific lists apply to distribution channels, seasonal lists support seasonal pricing strategies, and clearance lists support inventory liquidation.']
  price_protection_days varchar [note: 'Number of days of price protection coverage offered under this price list. Defines the lookback period for retroactive price adjustments when price protection is enabled.']
  price_protection_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether price protection provisions apply to this price list. When true, customers who purchased at higher prices may be eligible for retroactive credits if prices decrease during the protection period.']
  price_rounding_rule varchar [note: 'Rounding rule applied to calculated prices from this list. Supports psychological pricing strategies and currency denomination constraints.']
  price_uom varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure in which prices are expressed in this list (e.g., EA for each, CS for case, PAL for pallet). Aligns with SAP MM material master UoM configuration.']
  pricing_condition_type varchar [note: 'SAP SD condition type code that defines how prices from this list are applied in pricing procedures (e.g., PR00 for base price, K004 for material price). Links price list to SAP pricing schema configuration.']
  rsp_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price list contains recommended selling prices (RSP) for retail partners. True when prices represent manufacturer-suggested retail pricing guidance.']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'SAP SD sales organization code to which this price list applies. Defines the organizational scope for pricing applicability in the sales and distribution structure.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system from which this price list record originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA SD, Oracle Pricing Cloud, Salesforce CPQ). Supports data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  tax_classification varchar [note: 'Tax category or classification code applicable to prices in this list. Determines tax treatment for products priced under this list in compliance with jurisdictional tax regulations.']
  trade_class varchar [note: 'Customer trade class or customer pricing group to which this price list applies. Used to segment pricing by customer type such as mass merchandisers, drug stores, grocery chains, convenience stores, or specialty retailers.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the price list. Increments with each revision to support version control and change tracking for pricing history and audit purposes.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this price list record. Required for audit trail and accountability in pricing governance.']

  Note: 'Standard price list master defining list prices, RSP/MSRP, and channel-specific base prices for SKUs across different trade classes and geographies. Contains both header-level metadata (price list code, name, currency, effective dates, channel applicability, type, approval status) and SKU-level price entries (unit price, MOQ, price UoM, effective dates, override flags) for each product in the list. Serves as the pricing baseline from which customer-specific pricing agreements and trade discounts are derived in SAP SD condition records.'
}

Table sales.price_list_item {
  price_list_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the price list item record. Primary key for this entity.']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent price list that contains this item. Links this SKU-level price entry to its governing price list header.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product (SKU) for which this price is defined. Identifies the specific finished good or SKU being priced.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval workflow status for this price list item. Tracks whether the price has been reviewed and approved by pricing management.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the person who approved this price list item. Supports audit trail and accountability for pricing decisions.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this price list item was approved. Part of the pricing governance audit trail.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'The sales channel or distribution channel for which this price applies (e.g., retail, wholesale, DTC, e-commerce). Enables channel-specific pricing strategies.']
  condition_type varchar [note: 'The SAP SD condition type code that defines the pricing logic for this item (e.g., PR00 for base price, K004 for customer discount). Maps to SAP pricing procedure.']
  cost_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The standard cost or COGS for this SKU in the specified currency. Used for margin analysis and profitability calculations. Business-confidential data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this price list item record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all price amounts in this record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'The customer segment or pricing group for which this price applies (e.g., national accounts, regional chains, independent retailers). Supports segment-based pricing.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'The SAP distribution channel code for this price list item. Defines the route-to-market for which this price applies.']
  division varchar [note: 'The SAP division code for this price list item. Represents the product line or business unit responsible for this pricing.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this price list item expires and is no longer valid for new sales transactions. Null indicates open-ended validity.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this price list item becomes active and can be used in sales transactions. Part of the item\'s validity period.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'The geographic region, country, or market for which this price is valid. Supports regional pricing strategies and multi-country operations.']
  gtin varchar [note: 'The globally unique GTIN (UPC, EAN, or GTIN-14) for the product. Supports EDI transactions and retail POS integration.']
  item_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential line number of this item within the parent price list. Used for ordering and display purposes.']
  list_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The base list price for this SKU in the specified currency and unit of measure. Represents the standard selling price before any discounts or promotions.']
  margin_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target gross margin percentage for this price list item, calculated as (list_price - cost_price) / list_price * 100. Business-confidential data.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(10,3) [note: 'The minimum quantity that must be ordered for this SKU at this price. Used to enforce order minimums and support volume-based pricing tiers.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the person who last modified this price list item record. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this price list item record was last modified. Part of the audit trail for tracking changes.']
  moq_uom varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the minimum order quantity (e.g., EA, CS, PAL).']
  msrp_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The manufacturer\'s suggested retail price for this SKU. Used for retail channel pricing guidance and price protection calculations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this price list item. Used to document special pricing conditions, exceptions, or business rationale.']
  override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price list item represents a manual override of standard pricing logic. True if this is an exception price.']
  price_list_item_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this price list item. Controls whether the item can be used in pricing calculations.']
  price_uom varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for which the price is quoted (e.g., EA for each, CS for case, PAL for pallet). Aligns with product packaging hierarchy.']
  pricing_unit_quantity decimal(10,3) [note: 'The quantity of base units represented by one pricing unit. For example, if price is per case and a case contains 12 units, this value is 12.']
  promotional_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price is part of a promotional campaign. True if this is a temporary promotional price rather than standard list price.']
  rsp_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The recommended selling price for this SKU in the target market. May differ from MSRP based on regional pricing strategies.']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'The SAP sales organization code responsible for this price list item. Aligns with organizational hierarchy and reporting structure.']
  sap_condition_record_number varchar [note: 'The unique SAP SD condition record number for this price list item. Enables traceability back to the source ERP system.']
  scale_basis varchar [note: 'The basis for volume-based pricing scales (e.g., quantity, order value, weight, volume). Defines how tiered pricing thresholds are measured.']
  scale_quantity_from decimal(10,3) [note: 'The lower bound of the quantity range for which this price applies in a tiered pricing structure. Used for volume discount calculations.']
  scale_quantity_to decimal(10,3) [note: 'The upper bound of the quantity range for which this price applies in a tiered pricing structure. Null indicates no upper limit for the highest tier.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the person who created this price list item record. Supports audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Individual SKU-level price entry within a price list, capturing the specific list price or MSRP/RSP for a given product in a given channel and currency. Records SKU/GTIN reference, price list reference, unit price, minimum order quantity (MOQ), price unit of measure, effective dates, and override flag. Enables granular SKU-level pricing management and supports SAP SD condition record maintenance.'
}

Table quality.inspection_plan {
  inspection_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inspection plan. Primary key for the inspection plan master data entity.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Inspection plans are designed to fulfill specific regulatory compliance obligations (e.g., GMP testing mandates, pharmacopoeial requirements). QA managers link each plan to the obligation it satisfies']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Inspection plans define resource requirements (sample_size, test_equipment_required, inspection_lead_time_days) budgeted against a cost center. Consumer goods quality finance planning requires linking']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_facility. Business justification: DC-specific Inspection Planning: inspection plans for inbound goods can be scoped to specific distribution centers with different AQL levels, sampling procedures, or regulatory requirements by market.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand Quality Assurance Plan links inspection plans to specific brand for compliance reporting and marketing claim validation.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material (raw material, packaging component, intermediate, or finished good) that this inspection plan applies to.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this inspection plan was formally approved for use. Part of the plan\'s audit trail and regulatory documentation.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether inspection results under this plan require formal approval by a quality manager or authorized person before batch release. Typically true for GMP-critical inspections.']
  aql_level decimal(5,2) [note: 'Acceptable Quality Level expressed as a percentage. Maximum percent defective that is considered acceptable for the inspection plan. Used in acceptance sampling decisions.']
  certificate_of_analysis_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a formal Certificate of Analysis document must be generated upon successful completion of inspections under this plan. Typically required for finished goods and customer-facing materials.']
  change_control_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the change control request or document that authorized the creation or last modification of this inspection plan. Required for GMP compliance and audit trail.']
  characteristic_count varchar [note: 'Number of quality characteristics (test parameters) defined in this inspection plan. Each characteristic has its own specification limits.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inspection plan record was first created in the source system. Part of the audit trail.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this inspection plan is no longer valid. Nullable for open-ended plans. Used for version control and regulatory compliance.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this inspection plan becomes valid and applicable for quality inspections. Part of the plan\'s effective date range.']
  gmp_critical_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inspection plan covers GMP-critical quality attributes that directly impact product safety, efficacy, or regulatory compliance. GMP-critical inspections require enhanced documentation and cannot be skipped.']
  inspection_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Expected number of calendar days required to complete all inspections and testing defined in this plan. Used for production scheduling and inventory planning.']
  inspection_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to perform the inspection: manual measurement, automated testing, laboratory analysis, visual examination, destructive testing, or non-destructive testing.']
  inspection_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of inspection based on supplier quality history and risk assessment. Tightened inspection applies stricter acceptance criteria.']
  inspection_stage varchar [note: 'Production or supply chain stage at which this inspection is triggered. Aligns with GMP critical control points.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Type of quality inspection this plan governs: incoming (supplier materials), in-process (production intermediates), final (finished goods), stability (shelf-life studies), release (batch release), or periodic (routine surveillance).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this inspection plan record. Used for change tracking and data synchronization.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the material subject to inspection. Determines applicable GMP requirements and inspection rigor.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this inspection plan. GMP requires regular review of quality procedures to ensure continued appropriateness.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the inspection plan, used across quality management systems and documentation. Externally visible code for referencing the plan in quality specifications and batch records.']
  plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the inspection plan including scope, objectives, and special instructions for quality control personnel.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the inspection plan describing its purpose and scope.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection plan. Only active plans are used for inspection lot creation.']
  regulatory_requirement varchar [note: 'Reference to the regulatory requirement, standard, or guideline that mandates this inspection (e.g., FDA 21 CFR Part 211, EU GMP Annex 1, ISO 22716). Critical for compliance audits.']
  responsible_inspector_role varchar [note: 'Job role or qualification level required for personnel authorized to perform inspections under this plan (e.g., QC Technician, QC Analyst, QC Manager). Ensures competency requirements are met.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number of this inspection plan. Incremented each time the plan is updated. Part of change control and audit trail.']
  sample_size varchar [note: 'Number of sample units to be drawn per inspection lot. Determined by statistical sampling plan and AQL requirements.']
  sample_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the sample (e.g., pieces, kg, liters, containers). Defines what constitutes one sample unit.']
  sampling_procedure_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the sampling procedure to be followed: sample size, sampling frequency, sampling method (random, systematic, stratified). References approved sampling SOP.']
  skip_lot_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether skip-lot inspection (reduced frequency based on consistent quality history) is permitted for this plan. Not allowed for GMP-critical inspections.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system from which this inspection plan record originated (e.g., SAP QM, Veeva Vault). Used for data lineage and troubleshooting.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this inspection plan in the source operational system. Enables traceability back to the system of record.']
  specification_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the quality specification document that this inspection plan implements. Ensures traceability to approved specifications.']
  test_equipment_required varchar [note: 'List or description of test equipment, instruments, or laboratory apparatus required to execute this inspection plan. Ensures resource availability.']

  Note: 'Master data defining QC inspection plans and quality specifications for raw materials, in-process intermediates, packaging, and finished goods. Captures sampling procedures, inspection characteristics with specification limits (USL/LSL/target), acceptable quality levels (AQL), test methods, units of measure, effective date ranges, and GMP-mandated control points. Serves as the single authoritative source for what to test, how to sample, and what limits apply — governing all inspection lot execution and feeding CoA generation. Aligned with SAP QM inspection planning.'
}

Table quality.inspection_lot {
  inspection_lot_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inspection lot. Primary key for the inspection lot entity representing a discrete QC inspection event triggered by production batch, goods receipt, or periodic review.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.batch_record. Business justification: Required for traceability: each inspection lot must be linked to the specific manufacturing batch record that produced the lot, enabling batch-level quality release decisions.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost-of-quality reporting in consumer goods requires attributing inspection labor, sampling, and overhead costs to a cost center. Finance teams use inspection_lot cost center attribution for quality c']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_facility. Business justification: Inbound Quality Inspection at DC: inspection lots are executed at specific distribution centers. DC-level quality performance reporting and GMP compliance audits require knowing which facility perform']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Goods receipt triggers inspection lot creation in standard QM/MM integration (SAP pattern). Traceability from inspection lot back to the specific GR document is required for stock posting, three-way m']
  inbound_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.inbound_receipt. Business justification: Goods Receipt Quality Inspection: when inbound goods arrive at a DC, an inspection lot is created against that receipt. QA managers need to trace every inspection lot back to the triggering inbound re']
  inspection_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the inspection plan template applied to this lot. Defines the characteristics to be inspected, sampling procedures, and acceptance criteria.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Regulatory traceability report requires linking each inspection lot to its originating lot batch for audit trails.']
  po_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line. Business justification: Inspection lots are created per PO line item (specific material and quantity). Linking to po_line enables per-line quality tracking, rejection quantity reconciliation against ordered quantity, and sup']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Required for traceability: link each inspection lot to the purchase order that supplied the material, enabling regulatory compliance and release decisions.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the material (raw material, semi-finished good, or finished good) being inspected in this lot.']
  stock_movement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_movement. Business justification: Quality inspection results trigger stock movements (goods receipt posting, transfer to unrestricted, scrapping of rejected material). Standard SAP QM/MM integration in consumer goods: inspection_lot d']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Quality inspection blocks stock in the stock_position (quality_inspection_quantity, quarantine_flag). Linking inspection_lot to stock_position enables QC teams to manage blocked-stock transitions and ']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier or vendor for goods receipt inspection lots. Used for supplier quality performance tracking.']
  supplier_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_site. Business justification: Incoming inspection lots must be traceable to the specific supplier manufacturing site for site-level quality performance tracking, audit scheduling, and regulatory compliance. Consumer goods companie']
  certificate_of_analysis_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the Certificate of Analysis issued for this inspection lot. Links to formal quality documentation in Veeva Vault.']
  coa_issue_date date [note: 'Date when the Certificate of Analysis was formally issued for this inspection lot.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection lot record was first created in the quality management system. Audit trail field.']
  decision_date date [note: 'Date when the formal usage decision was made and recorded in the quality management system.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the usage decision was recorded. Used for audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  defect_count varchar [note: 'Total number of defects or non-conformances identified during inspection. Used for quality metrics and trend analysis.']
  disposition_outcome varchar [note: 'Final outcome status of the lot after disposition decision and stock posting. Confirms the actual inventory movement executed.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration or best-before date assigned to the batch based on stability study results and shelf-life specifications.']
  gmp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the inspection lot was produced and inspected in compliance with Good Manufacturing Practice regulations.']
  inspection_end_date date [note: 'Date when all inspection and testing activities were completed for this lot.']
  inspection_location varchar [note: 'Physical location or quality lab where the inspection was performed. May be different from the plant code for centralized testing facilities.']
  inspection_lot_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the inspection lot. Externally visible alphanumeric code used in SAP QM and quality documentation for traceability and audit purposes.']
  inspection_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for inspector comments, observations, or special conditions noted during the inspection process.']
  inspection_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the inspection lot. Determines the sequence and urgency of inspection execution in the quality lab.']
  inspection_start_date date [note: 'Date when the physical inspection and testing activities began for this lot.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection lot. Tracks progression from creation through inspection execution to final disposition decision.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inspection lot based on the triggering event. Determines the inspection plan template and sampling strategy applied.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection lot record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change for audit and compliance purposes.']
  lot_origin_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the originating transaction (production order number, purchase order number, delivery number, etc.) that triggered this inspection lot.']
  lot_origin_type varchar [note: 'Source document type that triggered the creation of this inspection lot. Links the quality inspection to the originating business transaction.']
  lot_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of material in the inspection lot. Represents the population from which samples are drawn for inspection.']
  lot_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the lot quantity (each, kilogram, liter, meter, etc.). Aligns with material master UOM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|KG|L|M|M2|M3|BOX|CASE — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  material_number varchar [note: 'Business material code (SKU) of the product being inspected. Used for cross-reference and reporting.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or facility code where the inspection is performed. Aligns with SAP plant master data.']
  regulatory_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lot is under regulatory hold pending additional review or approval from regulatory affairs or quality assurance.']
  regulatory_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the lot is under regulatory hold. Documents the specific compliance or quality concern requiring additional review.']
  retest_date date [note: 'Date when the material must be re-inspected to confirm continued compliance with quality specifications. Applicable for raw materials and intermediates.']
  sample_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material drawn as a sample for inspection testing. Determined by the inspection plan sampling procedure.']
  sample_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the sample quantity drawn for testing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|KG|L|M|M2|M3|BOX|CASE — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  stock_posting_instruction varchar [note: 'Instruction for inventory movement based on the usage decision. Determines the target stock type for material posting in warehouse management.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Warehouse storage location code where the inspected material is held during quality inspection.']
  usage_decision varchar [note: 'Final disposition decision for the inspected lot. Determines whether material can be released for use, must be reworked, or should be rejected.']
  usage_decision_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the usage decision. Used for automated stock posting and reporting in SAP QM.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a QC inspection lot triggered by a production batch, goods receipt, or periodic review. Tracks lot origin (batch, purchase order, production order), inspection type, assigned inspection plan, lot quantity, inspection status, and complete disposition lifecycle including formal usage decision (accept/reject/restricted use/rework), decision maker, decision date, decision code, stock posting instruction, regulatory hold flag, and disposition outcome. Core operational entity in SAP QM representing the end-to-end inspection-to-disposition workflow.'
}

Table quality.inspection_result {
  inspection_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inspection result record. Primary key for granular QC (Quality Control) measurement and test result records captured at the characteristic level within an inspection lot.']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Inspection results for incoming goods must be directly traceable to the goods receipt document for GR-based quality holds, three-way match validation, and regulatory traceability. Quality teams refere']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent inspection lot under which this characteristic-level result was captured. Links to the inspection lot entity that groups multiple inspection results for a batch or production run.']
  sample_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the physical sample from which this measurement was taken. Enables traceability back to the specific sample unit within the inspection lot.']
  specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Link inspection_result to its governing specification for easy access to spec limits and versioning.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of this inspection result by a quality supervisor or manager. Pending indicates awaiting review; approved indicates result accepted; rejected indicates result invalidated and retest required.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this inspection result was approved. Required for GMP audit trails and batch release decision traceability.']
  coa_inclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this result should be included in the Certificate of Analysis (COA) document for the batch. True indicates inclusion; false indicates internal-only result.']
  control_chart_rule_violated varchar [note: 'Description of the specific SPC control chart rule that was violated (e.g., point beyond control limit, 7 consecutive points above centerline, 2 of 3 points beyond 2-sigma). Populated when control_chart_violation_flag is true.']
  control_chart_violation_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this measurement violates SPC (Statistical Process Control) control chart rules (e.g., out of control limits, run rules, trend rules). True indicates a violation requiring investigation.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system from which this inspection result was captured (e.g., SAP QM, LIMS, Veeva Vault, manual entry). Used for data lineage and audit purposes.']
  defect_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code classifying the type of defect or non-conformance observed when result_status is fail. Used for defect trending and root cause analysis.']
  defect_severity varchar [note: 'Classification of the severity of the non-conformance. Critical defects pose safety or regulatory risk; major defects affect functionality; minor defects are cosmetic or non-functional.']
  deviation_code varchar [note: 'Reference to an approved deviation or waiver document that authorizes acceptance of a non-conforming result. Populated when result_status is waived.']
  environmental_condition_humidity decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity (percentage) at the time of measurement. Critical for tests sensitive to moisture levels.']
  environmental_condition_temperature decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ambient temperature (in degrees Celsius) at the time of measurement. Critical for tests sensitive to environmental conditions.']
  inspection_characteristic_code varchar [note: 'Unique code identifying the specific quality characteristic being measured (e.g., pH level, viscosity, weight, color, microbial count). Corresponds to the master inspection characteristic definition.']
  inspection_characteristic_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the quality characteristic being tested (e.g., Product pH, Net Weight, Microbial Load, Color Consistency).']
  inspection_method_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the standardized test method or procedure used to perform this measurement (e.g., ASTM method, USP method, internal SOP number).']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this specific characteristic measurement was performed. Critical for time-sensitive tests and GMP audit trails.']
  instrument_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the measurement instrument or equipment used to capture this result (e.g., balance ID, pH meter ID, spectrophotometer ID). Required for GMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) audit trails and instrument calibration traceability.']
  laboratory_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the laboratory or testing facility where this measurement was performed. May be an internal QC lab or an external contract lab.']
  lower_specification_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The minimum acceptable value for this characteristic as defined in the product specification or quality standard. Values below this threshold indicate non-conformance.']
  measured_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The actual quantitative measurement or test result value recorded during inspection. For qualitative characteristics, this may be encoded numerically or stored as a code.']
  measurement_uncertainty decimal(18,6) [note: 'Quantified estimate of the uncertainty or error margin associated with the measured value. Required for ISO 17025 compliance and advanced SPC (Statistical Process Control) analysis.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this inspection result record was first created in the system. Audit trail field for data governance.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this inspection result record was last modified. Audit trail field for data governance and change tracking.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this result must be reported to regulatory authorities (e.g., FDA, EPA) due to non-conformance or safety concerns. True indicates reportable event.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or observations recorded by the inspector regarding this measurement. May include notes on sample condition, procedural deviations, or contextual information.']
  result_status varchar [note: 'Pass/fail status of this individual characteristic measurement. Pass indicates the measured value is within specification limits; fail indicates non-conformance; conditional indicates acceptance with restrictions; pending indicates awaiting review; waived indicates non-conformance accepted by authorized deviation.']
  retest_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this result is a retest of a previously failed or inconclusive measurement. True indicates this is a retest; false indicates initial test.']
  retest_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for retesting this characteristic. Applicable only when retest_indicator is true.']
  sample_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of this sample within the inspection lot (e.g., 1, 2, 3). Used to track sampling order and support statistical sampling plans.']
  target_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The ideal or nominal value for this characteristic. Used for process capability analysis and SPC (Statistical Process Control) charting.']
  test_batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or run number of the test reagents or consumables used during this measurement. Required for traceability in regulated environments.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the measured value is expressed (e.g., grams, milliliters, pH units, CFU/g for colony forming units per gram, percentage).']
  upper_specification_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The maximum acceptable value for this characteristic as defined in the product specification or quality standard. Values above this threshold indicate non-conformance.']

  Note: 'Granular QC measurement and test result records captured at the characteristic level within an inspection lot. Stores measured values, upper/lower specification limits, pass/fail status, instrument used, inspector ID, and timestamp. Supports SPC (Statistical Process Control) and GMP audit trails per ISO 9001 and GMP requirements.'
}

Table quality.usage_decision {
  usage_decision_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the quality control usage decision record. Primary key.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the CAPA record initiated as a result of this usage decision, if applicable. Null if no CAPA was required.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Usage decisions (accept/reject/rework) directly drive financial postings: rejections trigger inventory write-offs, rework triggers cost postings. Consumer goods finance requires cost center attributio']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_facility. Business justification: DC-level Disposition Management: usage decisions (accept/reject/rework) made at distribution facilities must reference the DC for inventory disposition execution and WMS stock posting. plant_code is a']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Usage decision (accept/reject/rework) is executed against a specific goods receipt document to trigger stock posting and GR reversal. Standard SAP QM workflow: usage decision references the GR to upda']
  inbound_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.inbound_receipt. Business justification: Inbound Disposition Reporting: usage decisions made after inspecting inbound receipts at DCs must reference the receipt for end-to-end inbound quality disposition tracking. Supplier scorecards and rec']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inspection lot for which this usage decision was made. Links to the QC inspection lot that was evaluated.']
  logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment. Business justification: Usage decisions (accept/reject/rework) directly drive logistics actions — return-to-vendor shipments, destruction movements, or release-for-distribution. Linking usage_decision to logistics_shipment e']
  po_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line. Business justification: Usage decision rejection quantities must reconcile against the specific PO line to update outstanding/invoiced quantities and trigger accounts payable deductions. Procurement teams require this link f']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material or product that was inspected and for which the usage decision applies.']
  stock_movement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_movement. Business justification: Usage decision posts a stock movement (material document) in ERP — accepted stock to unrestricted, rejected to scrap/returns. Consumer goods QM standard: usage_decision.stock_posting_instruction resol']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Usage decision (accept/reject/rework) directly drives stock posting to unrestricted, blocked, or scrap stock positions. This is the core QM-to-MM integration in consumer goods ERP; the link enables au']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor who provided the material, if applicable. Used for supplier quality management and vendor scorecarding.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date when the usage decision was formally approved by authorized quality management personnel. Null if no approval was required.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this usage decision requires additional management or quality assurance approval before batch release. True=Approval required, False=No additional approval needed.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The production batch or lot number to which this usage decision applies. Critical for traceability and recall management.']
  capa_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this usage decision triggers a CAPA process due to significant non-conformance. True=CAPA required, False=No CAPA needed. Links to continuous improvement.']
  certificate_of_analysis_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the Certificate of Analysis document that supports this usage decision. Links to detailed test results and specifications.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional free-text comments or notes related to the usage decision. May include special handling instructions, escalation notes, or contextual information.']
  decision_code varchar [note: 'The formal quality control usage decision code. A=Accept (Unrestricted Use), R=Reject, Q=Quality Hold, S=Restricted Use (Sample/Scrap), X=Rework Required. Drives downstream inventory disposition.']
  decision_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the usage decision was formally made and recorded. Critical for compliance timelines and batch release tracking.']
  decision_maker_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the quality control inspector or authorized personnel who made the usage decision. Captured for audit and traceability purposes.']
  decision_text varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description or rationale for the usage decision. Captures the quality assessor\'s reasoning and any special conditions or instructions.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the usage decision was recorded in the system. Provides audit trail precision for regulatory compliance.']
  defect_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the primary defect or non-conformance that led to rejection or restricted use decision. Used for root cause analysis and trend reporting.']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the defect, non-conformance, or quality issue identified during inspection. Supports CAPA and continuous improvement processes.']
  disposition_date date [note: 'The date when the material disposition action was completed (e.g., stock posted to unrestricted, blocked, or scrapped). Null if disposition is still pending.']
  disposition_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the material disposition action following the usage decision. pending=Decision made but stock not yet moved, completed=Stock posting completed, cancelled=Decision reversed or voided.']
  gmp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch meets Good Manufacturing Practice requirements. True=GMP compliant, False=Non-compliant. Required for pharmaceutical and cosmetic products.']
  inspection_type varchar [note: 'The type of quality inspection that led to this usage decision. goods_receipt=incoming material inspection, in_process=during production, final=finished goods, stability=shelf-life testing, complaint=customer complaint investigation.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'The manufacturing plant or facility code where the inspection and usage decision were performed. Critical for multi-site quality management.']
  quantity_accepted decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of material accepted for unrestricted use based on the usage decision. Measured in the material\'s base unit of measure.']
  quantity_rejected decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of material rejected and not suitable for use. Measured in the material\'s base unit of measure. May trigger disposal or return to supplier.']
  quantity_rework decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of material designated for rework or reprocessing to meet quality standards. Measured in the material\'s base unit of measure.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this usage decision record was first created in the lakehouse. Part of the audit trail for data governance.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this usage decision record was last updated in the lakehouse. Tracks data freshness and change history.']
  regulatory_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is under regulatory hold and cannot be released for distribution. True=Hold in effect, False=No regulatory hold. Critical for FDA and EPA compliance.']
  regulatory_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the material is under regulatory hold. May reference specific test failures, compliance issues, or pending regulatory approvals.']
  sampling_procedure varchar [note: 'The sampling method or procedure used during the inspection (e.g., random sampling, statistical sampling, 100% inspection). Ensures inspection validity.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system from which this usage decision record originated (e.g., SAP QM, Veeva Vault, Siemens Opcenter MES). Used for data lineage and integration tracking.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this usage decision record in the source operational system. Enables traceability back to the system of record.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the quantities in this usage decision (e.g., EA=Each, KG=Kilogram, L=Liter, CS=Case). Aligns with material master UOM.']

  Note: 'Records the formal QC usage decision made at the conclusion of an inspection lot — accept, reject, restricted use, or rework. Captures decision code, decision maker, decision date, stock posting instruction, and regulatory hold flag. Drives downstream batch release and inventory disposition in SAP QM/MM.'
}

This FK normalizes the relationship between the quality event record and the inspection lot that surfaced the']

Business justification: Brand managers in consumer goods must track nonconformance events by brand for brand crisis management, brand-level quality KPI dashboards, and regulatory reporting. A contamination or defect event on']

Business justification: Outbound Fulfillment Nonconformance: nonconformances detected during pick/pack operations (e.g., damaged goods, wrong lot picked) must reference the outbound order. Order-level quality defect tracking']
  po_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line. Business justification: Nonconformances on incoming materials must reference the PO line to enable contract penalty clause enforcement, supplier debit memo generation, and procurement reporting on defect rates by purchased m']

Business justification: Nonconformances detected at retail (shelf damage, temperature excursion, mislabeling) must reference the specific store for field quality investigations and corrective actions. \'Retail field quality n']

Business justification: Regulatory-reportable nonconformances (regulatory_reportable_flag exists on nonconformance) require formal regulatory submissions. Linking the nonconformance to its regulatory submission supports mand']

Business justification: A nonconformance is often initiated as a direct consequence of a reject or conditional usage decision. Linking nonconformance.usage_decision_id → usage_decision.usage_decision_id establishes the forma']
  affected_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of product units or material affected by the nonconformance. Used for containment scope and financial impact assessment.']
