  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the nonconformance was formally closed after verification of corrective actions and effectiveness checks. Null if still open.']
  containment_action varchar [note: 'Immediate containment actions taken to prevent further occurrence or distribution of nonconforming product. Describes short-term corrective measures implemented before root cause analysis.']
  containment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when containment actions were completed and verified effective. Critical for regulatory reporting and OTIF compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this nonconformance record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer. Audit trail for data governance.']
  defect_classification varchar [note: 'Technical classification of the defect type: appearance (cosmetic defect), functionality (performance issue), safety (consumer safety risk), labeling (incorrect or missing label information), packaging (packaging integrity issue), contamination (foreign material or microbial contamination), specification_failure (out-of-specification test result), or process_deviation (manufacturing process non-conformance). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: appearance|functionality|safety|labeling|packaging|contamination|specification_failure|process_deviation — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  defect_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the nonconformance, including observed symptoms, affected characteristics, and initial assessment. Captures the what, where, and when of the quality event.']
  detected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the nonconformance was first detected or observed. Represents the real-world event time of quality issue identification.']
  detection_source varchar [note: 'Origin point where the nonconformance was first identified: QC inspection (quality control laboratory testing), manufacturing line (in-process detection), supplier receipt (incoming material inspection), customer complaint (consumer-reported issue), regulatory audit (FDA or regulatory body inspection), internal audit (GMP self-audit), or stability study (long-term product stability testing). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: qc_inspection|manufacturing_line|supplier_receipt|customer_complaint|regulatory_audit|internal_audit|stability_study — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  disposition_approved_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the quality authority who approved the disposition decision. Links to workforce/employee master data for audit trail and GMP compliance.']
  disposition_approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the disposition decision was formally approved by quality authority. Critical for GMP audit trail and batch release decisions.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final disposition decision for the nonconforming product or material: use_as_is (approved for use despite nonconformance), rework (reprocess to meet specifications), scrap (destroy), return_to_supplier (send back to vendor), downgrade (sell as lower grade), or quarantine (hold pending further investigation).']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the quality event: complaint (customer-reported issue), defect (internal manufacturing defect), deviation (process deviation from standard), improvement request (continuous improvement opportunity), supplier issue (supplier quality problem), or audit finding (GMP audit observation).']
  financial_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual financial impact of the nonconformance, including costs of rework, scrap, containment, investigation, customer credits, and potential recall costs. Expressed in base currency.']
  financial_impact_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the financial impact amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  nonconformance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the nonconformance record: open (newly created), in_investigation (root cause analysis underway), containment_active (immediate containment actions in progress), capa_assigned (corrective and preventive action assigned), closed (resolved and verified), or cancelled (invalidated).']
  notification_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier for the quality notification or nonconformance event. Corresponds to SAP QM notification number or Veeva Vault quality event identifier.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority for resolution based on operational impact, customer visibility, and regulatory risk. Urgent: immediate action required; High: expedited resolution needed; Medium: standard timeline; Low: routine handling.']
  regulatory_report_date date [note: 'Date when the nonconformance was reported to regulatory authorities, if applicable. Null if not reportable or not yet reported.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this nonconformance requires reporting to regulatory authorities (FDA, EPA, CPSC) based on severity, product category, and regulatory requirements. True indicates mandatory regulatory notification.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the nonconformance was formally logged into the quality management system (SAP QM or Veeva Vault). May differ from detection timestamp due to reporting lag.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Functional department responsible for investigating and resolving the nonconformance (e.g., Quality Assurance, Manufacturing, Supply Chain, R&D).']
  responsible_plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or facility code where the nonconformance originated or is being managed. Links to distribution_facility for organizational accountability.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Detailed findings from root cause investigation, including methodology used (5-Why, Fishbone, Fault Tree Analysis) and identified root causes. Supports CAPA development.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the quality event based on impact to product safety, efficacy, and regulatory compliance. Critical: immediate risk to consumer safety or regulatory violation; Major: significant quality impact requiring containment; Minor: low impact quality issue.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of record for this nonconformance: SAP_QM (SAP Quality Management module), VEEVA_VAULT (Veeva Vault Quality), or MANUAL_ENTRY (direct data entry).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this nonconformance record in the source system (SAP QM notification number or Veeva Vault quality event ID). Enables traceability back to operational system.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this nonconformance record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer. Audit trail for data governance and change tracking.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify effectiveness of corrective actions and confirm the nonconformance has been resolved (e.g., re-inspection, re-testing, process validation, trend analysis).']

  Note: 'Unified quality event record for non-conformances, deviations, quality notifications, and improvement requests identified during manufacturing, QC inspection, supplier receipt, or customer complaint escalation. Captures event type (complaint, defect, deviation, improvement request), defect description, defect classification, affected batch/SKU, detection source, severity, priority, containment actions, task assignments, responsible plant, and linkage to CAPA. Serves as the single routing point for all quality events across SAP QM notifications and Veeva Vault workflows. Aligns with ISO 9001:2015 clause 10.2 and GMP deviation reporting requirements.'
}

Table quality.capa {
  capa_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the CAPA record. Primary key.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.compliance_obligation. Business justification: CAPA actions are often initiated to meet specific compliance obligations; linking ties corrective actions to the obligation.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: CAPA cost impact must be charged to a cost center for budgeting and expense analysis.']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_facility. Business justification: Distribution CAPA Management: CAPAs initiated for DC-level issues (temperature excursions, handling damage patterns, storage violations) must reference the responsible distribution facility. DC qualit']
  logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment. Business justification: CAPAs in consumer goods are routinely initiated from logistics events — cold chain breaches, repeated OTIF failures, or in-transit damage patterns. Linking capa to logistics_shipment enables carrier p']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: CAPA Impact Report tracks corrective actions affecting brand reputation, used by marketing for risk communication and messaging.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the product affected by or related to this CAPA. Links to the product master data.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: CAPAs with regulatory_reportable_flag require formal regulatory submissions (e.g., field safety corrective action reports, deviation notifications to authorities). Linking CAPA to its regulatory submi']
  supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_contract. Business justification: CAPA actions often stem from supplier contract obligations; linking ensures contract compliance tracking and auditability of corrective measures.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier associated with the CAPA, if the issue originated from supplier-provided materials or services.']
  supplier_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_site. Business justification: CAPAs raised from supplier quality failures must be traceable to the specific supplier site where the issue originated. Site-level CAPA tracking is required for supplier audit management, re-qualifica']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'The actual date when all CAPA actions were completed and verified, prior to formal closure.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch or lot number associated with the CAPA, if applicable. Used to trace quality issues to specific production runs.']
  capa_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the nonconformance, audit finding, complaint, or stability failure that triggered the CAPA.']
  capa_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the CAPA record, typically formatted as CAPA-NNNNNN. Used for external communication and audit trails.']
  capa_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the CAPA record, tracking progression from initiation through closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|investigation|action_plan|implementation|verification|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  capa_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the CAPA as corrective (addressing existing nonconformance), preventive (preventing potential nonconformance), or combined (both corrective and preventive actions).']
  closure_comments varchar [note: 'Final comments or notes recorded at the time of CAPA closure, summarizing outcomes and lessons learned.']
  closure_date date [note: 'The date when the CAPA was formally closed after successful completion and verification of all actions.']
  corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective action(s) taken to address the existing nonconformance and eliminate its cause.']
  cost_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual financial cost impact of the CAPA issue, including costs of investigation, rework, scrap, recall, or other remediation activities.']
  cost_impact_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost impact amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CAPA record was first created in the quality management system.']
  customer_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the CAPA issue had a direct impact on customers, such as product quality defects, delivery delays, or safety concerns.']
  effectiveness_verification_method varchar [note: 'The method or approach used to verify that the corrective and preventive actions were effective in resolving the issue and preventing recurrence (e.g., follow-up audit, process monitoring, trend analysis).']
  effectiveness_verification_result varchar [note: 'The outcome of the effectiveness verification, indicating whether the CAPA actions successfully resolved the issue and prevented recurrence.']
  gmp_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the CAPA issue involved a deviation from Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) standards.']
  initiated_date date [note: 'The date when the CAPA record was formally initiated and opened in the quality management system.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the CAPA record was last updated or modified.']
  preventive_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the preventive action(s) implemented to prevent recurrence of the issue or occurrence of similar issues.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the CAPA for resource allocation and scheduling purposes.']
  process_area varchar [note: 'The specific process, department, or functional area where the issue occurred (e.g., Manufacturing, Packaging, Quality Control, Supply Chain).']
  regulatory_report_date date [note: 'The date when the CAPA issue was reported to the relevant regulatory authority, if applicable.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this CAPA issue is required to be reported to regulatory authorities such as FDA, EPA, or other governing bodies.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'The department or functional area responsible for implementing the CAPA actions.']
  root_cause_analysis_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to identify the root cause of the issue, such as 5 Whys, Fishbone Diagram, Fault Tree Analysis, Pareto Analysis, or Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA).']
  root_cause_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the identified root cause(s) of the nonconformance or issue.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the CAPA issue based on impact to product quality, safety, regulatory compliance, or business operations.']
  source_type varchar [note: 'The origin or trigger event that initiated the CAPA, such as nonconformance, audit finding, customer complaint, supplier quality issue, stability study failure, or regulatory inspection finding.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'The planned or target date by which all CAPA actions should be completed and verified.']
  title varchar [note: 'Brief descriptive title summarizing the CAPA issue or focus area.']
  verification_date date [note: 'The date when the effectiveness verification was completed.']

  Note: 'Corrective and Preventive Action (CAPA) records initiated in response to nonconformances, audit findings, complaints, or stability failures. Tracks root cause analysis method, corrective actions, preventive actions, responsible owner, target completion dates, effectiveness verification, and closure status. Managed in Veeva Vault QMS.'
}

Table quality.batch_release {
  batch_release_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the batch release decision record. Primary key for the batch release entity.']
  batch_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.batch_record. Business justification: Batch release decisions are based on the manufacturing batch record; linking ensures release status reflects the underlying production data and supports regulatory audit trails.']
  certificate_of_analysis_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.certificate_of_analysis. Business justification: Link batch_release to the Certificate of Analysis that documents the released batch, removing redundant reference number.']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_facility. Business justification: Batch Release by Distribution Center: in consumer goods, batch release authorization can be scoped to specific DCs for market-specific regulatory compliance. Qualified Person sign-off and regulatory m']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: For purchased/contract-manufactured materials, batch release is triggered after goods receipt and quality inspection. Linking batch_release to goods_receipt enables GMP traceability from release decis']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: A batch release decision is formally linked to the QC inspection lot that validated the batch. batch_release currently has sap_qm_inspection_lot (STRING) which is a denormalized string reference to th']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Batch release decisions rely on the exact lot batch record to confirm quality release status and regulatory compliance.']
  registration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.regulatory_registration. Business justification: GMP batch release requires a Qualified Person to certify a batch against the product\'s regulatory registration for the target market. \'product_registration_number\' and \'regulatory_market\' on batch_rel']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Batch release reports require linking each release to the specific SKU for traceability, regulatory filing, and inventory reconciliation.']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: QP-signed batch release moves stock from quality-hold to unrestricted in the stock_position. Consumer goods regulatory requirement: released_quantity and release_decision must be traceable to the spec']
  audit_trail_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the complete audit trail log for all actions and approvals related to this batch release. Required for 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.']
  batch_size_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of finished goods produced in this batch. Used for yield calculation and inventory planning.']
  batch_size_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the batch size quantity (e.g., kilograms, liters, units). Aligns with GS1 standards for trade item measurement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kg|lbs|liters|gallons|units|cases|pallets — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  capa_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a CAPA investigation is required based on deviations or non-conformances identified during batch production or testing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch release record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for regulatory compliance.']
  deviation_count varchar [note: 'Number of manufacturing or quality deviations recorded during the production and testing of this batch. Used for quality trend analysis.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the batch should not be sold or used. Calculated from production date plus shelf life. Critical for FEFO inventory management and regulatory compliance.']
  gmp_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the batch was manufactured in full compliance with GMP requirements. False indicates deviation or non-conformance.']
  hold_initiated_date date [note: 'Date when the regulatory or business hold was placed on the batch. Nullable if no hold exists.']
  hold_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the batch is on hold, if applicable. Includes regulatory citation, investigation reference, or customer complaint number.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the batch release record was last updated. Tracks changes to release status, hold status, or other critical fields.']
  manufacturing_site_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the manufacturing facility or plant where the batch was produced. Used for site-level quality tracking and regulatory reporting.']
  production_date date [note: 'Date when the batch manufacturing was completed. Critical for shelf-life calculation and FEFO inventory management.']
  qc_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Overall status of QC inspection and testing for this batch. Summarizes results from multiple QC test records.']
  qp_signature_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the Qualified Person electronically signed the batch release decision. Part of 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature compliance.']
  qualified_person_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the Qualified Person who signed off on the batch release. Required for regulatory audit trail.']
  regulatory_hold_status varchar [note: 'Indicates if the batch is under any regulatory or business hold that prevents distribution. Holds may be imposed by FDA, EPA, internal quality, customer complaint, or recall investigation.']
  rejected_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of the batch that was rejected and marked for destruction or rework. Part of quality yield tracking.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date when the batch was officially released for distribution and sale. Marks the point at which product becomes available to promise (ATP).']
  release_decision varchar [note: 'Final disposition decision for the batch: released for distribution, rejected for destruction, quarantine for further testing, conditional release with restrictions, pending review, or on hold pending investigation. Critical GMP control point.']
  release_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments from the Qualified Person regarding the release decision. May include justification for conditional release or special handling instructions.']
  release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the batch release decision was recorded in the system. Used for audit trail and regulatory compliance.']
  released_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of the batch that was approved for release and distribution. May be less than batch size if partial rejection occurred.']
  retest_date date [note: 'Date when the batch must be retested to confirm continued quality and stability. Common for raw materials and intermediates.']
  stability_study_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number linking to the stability study protocol and results that support the assigned shelf life and expiry date for this batch.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Document identifier in Veeva Vault system linking to the official batch release record and supporting quality documents. Integrates with Veeva Vault QMS.']

  Note: 'Formal batch release decision records authorizing finished goods batches for distribution and sale. Captures batch number, product SKU, release decision (released, rejected, quarantine), QP (Qualified Person) sign-off, release date, certificate of analysis reference, and regulatory hold status. Critical GMP control point per FDA 21 CFR and EU GMP.'
}

Business justification: Inbound COA Verification: COAs are received with inbound goods at DCs and must be verified against the receipt. Supplier quality management and GMP inbound inspection processes require linking the COA']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: A Certificate of Analysis is issued based on the test results from a specific inspection lot. inspection_lot currently has certificate_of_analysis_number (STRING) as a denormalized reference, but the ']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the lot or batch for which this certificate of analysis was issued. Links to the specific production batch of finished goods or raw materials.']
  po_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line. Business justification: A CoA is issued for a specific material quantity delivered on a PO line. This link enables direct CoA-to-PO-line traceability for supplier audits, regulatory submissions, and quality specification com']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: COA must reference the supplier of the batch for traceability and regulatory compliance; supplier_name is replaced by FK.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the certificate was officially approved by the authorized signatory. Represents the moment of quality release decision.']

  authorized_signatory_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the authorized signatory (e.g., QA Manager, Quality Director, Laboratory Supervisor).']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The manufacturing batch or lot number for the material being certified. Critical for traceability and recall management.']
  batch_size decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity produced in the manufacturing batch. Used to assess sample representativeness and for statistical quality control.']
  batch_size_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the batch size. Typically matches the material\'s production UOM.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique certificate number assigned to this CoA document. Used for customer communication and regulatory submissions.']

  document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the digitally signed PDF or scanned image of the certificate stored in Veeva Vault or document management system.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which the material should not be used or sold. Based on stability studies and regulatory requirements.']
  gmp_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the batch and testing process were conducted under GMP conditions. True if GMP-compliant.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which this certificate of analysis was officially issued and authorized for distribution to customers or regulatory bodies.']
  manufacturing_date date [note: 'The date on which the batch was manufactured or produced. Used for shelf-life calculations and FEFO inventory management.']
  manufacturing_site_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the manufacturing facility or plant where the batch was produced. Critical for site-specific compliance and traceability.']
  manufacturing_site_name varchar [note: 'Full name and location of the manufacturing site. Supports multi-site operations and regulatory site registration requirements.']
  material_code varchar [note: 'The product or material code (SKU, item number, or raw material identifier) for which this certificate was issued. Links to master product or material data.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the material or product covered by this certificate. Includes product name, variant, and key characteristics.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this certificate record was last modified. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  overall_result_status varchar [note: 'Aggregate pass/fail status for all test parameters on this certificate. Determines batch release decision and customer distribution eligibility.']
  quantity_tested decimal(18,3) [note: 'The quantity of material sampled and tested for this certificate. Expressed in the material\'s base unit of measure.']
  quantity_tested_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity tested (e.g., kilograms, liters, units). Aligns with material master UOM.']
  regulatory_submission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this certificate is required for regulatory submissions or compliance filings with FDA, EPA, or other governing bodies. True if required for submission.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, observations, or special conditions related to the testing or batch. May include deviations or special handling instructions.']
  retest_date date [note: 'The date by which the material should be retested to confirm continued conformance to specifications. Applicable primarily to raw materials.']
  specification_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the product specification or quality standard used for testing. Ensures traceability to the correct specification limits.']
  storage_condition varchar [note: 'Recommended storage conditions for the material (e.g., store in cool dry place, refrigerate at 2-8°C, protect from light). Based on stability data.']
  supplier_lot_number varchar [note: 'The lot or batch number assigned by the supplier for raw materials or packaging materials. Used for supplier quality traceability.']
  test_date date [note: 'The date on which quality control testing was performed for this certificate. May differ from manufacturing date for retained samples.']
  test_method_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the analytical test methods used (e.g., USP, ASTM, internal method codes). May be a comma-separated list for multiple methods.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Unique document identifier in Veeva Vault system where the certificate is stored. Enables integration with regulatory document management.']

  Note: 'Certificate of Analysis (CoA) documents issued per finished goods or raw material batch. Stores test parameters, specification limits, actual results, pass/fail status, issuing lab, authorized signatory, issue date, and customer-facing distribution flag. Supports trade customer requirements and regulatory submissions. Managed in Veeva Vault.'
}

}

Table quality.specification {
  specification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the quality specification record. Primary key for this entity.']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Each quality specification must satisfy a compliance obligation; linking ensures traceability to the governing regulation.']
  inspection_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SAP QM inspection plan that uses this specification. Links specification to operational quality control procedures.']
  specification_material_sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material or product this specification applies to. Links to the master material record in SAP MM.']
  specification_sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Quality specifications are defined per SKU to ensure manufacturing compliance and product release criteria.']
  superseded_by_specification_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer specification that replaces this one when status is superseded. Maintains specification version lineage.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this specification was formally approved for use. Part of the specification lifecycle audit trail.']
  coa_inclusion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether test results for this specification must be included on the Certificate of Analysis provided to customers. Drives CoA generation logic.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this specification record was first created in the system. Part of the audit trail for compliance and traceability.']
  critical_quality_attribute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this characteristic is a Critical Quality Attribute that directly impacts product safety, efficacy, or regulatory compliance. CQAs require enhanced controls and documentation.']
  customer_requirement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this specification is driven by specific customer contractual requirements rather than internal or regulatory standards.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date after which this specification is no longer valid. Null indicates an open-ended specification. Used for specification versioning and supersession.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this specification becomes valid and must be used for quality control testing. Supports time-based specification management.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this specification record was last updated. Tracks change history for audit and compliance purposes.']
  lower_specification_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The minimum acceptable value for the test characteristic. Results below this limit indicate non-conformance.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this specification. Used for compliance tracking and specification maintenance planning.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional context, special instructions, or clarifications related to this specification. May include testing conditions, sample preparation requirements, or interpretation guidance.']
  review_frequency_months varchar [note: 'How often this specification must be reviewed and revalidated, expressed in months. Ensures specifications remain current and appropriate.']
  sampling_plan varchar [note: 'Description of the sampling methodology and sample size requirements for this test (e.g., AQL 2.5, n=10, ANSI/ASQ Z1.4). Ensures statistical validity of test results.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system from which this specification record originated (e.g., SAP QM, Veeva Vault, PLM system). Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  specification_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the type of quality attribute being specified. Groups related test characteristics for reporting and analysis.']
  specification_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the quality specification, used for external reference and documentation. Unique across the organization.']
  specification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the specification. Only active specifications are used for quality control and Certificate of Analysis (CoA) generation.']
  specification_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the specification based on the material or product stage it applies to. Determines the applicable quality control procedures.']
  stability_indicating_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this test characteristic is used in stability studies to monitor product degradation over time. Critical for shelf-life determination.']
  target_value decimal(18,6) [note: 'The ideal or nominal value for the test characteristic. Represents the center point of the acceptable range.']
  test_characteristic varchar [note: 'The specific quality attribute or property being measured or tested (e.g., pH, viscosity, color, weight, purity, microbial count). Defines what is being inspected.']
  test_equipment_required varchar [note: 'Description of specialized equipment or instrumentation needed to perform this test (e.g., HPLC, spectrophotometer, viscometer). Supports resource planning and capability assessment.']
  test_frequency varchar [note: 'How often this test characteristic must be measured during production or quality control. Drives inspection plan scheduling. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: per_batch|per_lot|hourly|daily|weekly|monthly|on_demand — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  test_method_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the standard test method or procedure used to measure the characteristic (e.g., ASTM D1475, USP 621, internal SOP number). Ensures consistent testing methodology.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the test characteristic is measured (e.g., mg/L, %, pH units, cP, CFU/g). Critical for interpreting specification limits.']
  upper_specification_limit decimal(18,6) [note: 'The maximum acceptable value for the test characteristic. Results above this limit indicate non-conformance.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the controlled document in Veeva Vault that contains the full specification details and supporting documentation. Ensures document control and regulatory compliance.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the specification to track revisions and changes over time. Follows major.minor versioning convention.']

  Note: 'Master data defining quality specifications for raw materials, packaging, in-process intermediates, and finished goods. Stores specification type, test characteristic, unit of measure, target value, upper/lower specification limits (USL/LSL), test method reference, and effective date range. Serves as the authoritative specification repository linked to inspection plans and CoA generation.'
}

Table quality.notification {
  notification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the quality notification record. Primary key.']
  capa_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this quality notification to a formal CAPA record for corrective and preventive actions.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Quality notification financial impact is allocated to a cost center for expense reporting.']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_facility. Business justification: Distribution Quality Notification: quality notifications for DC issues (temperature deviations, handling damage, storage violations) must reference the facility. plant_name is a denormalized DC name t']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.goods_receipt. Business justification: Quality notifications for incoming goods defects are raised against the specific goods receipt document. This link enables automated GR hold, supplier notification workflows, and debit memo processing']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: SAP QM quality notifications are frequently created in direct response to inspection lot failures. notification currently has no structured FK to inspection_lot despite being the formal routing mechan']
  logistics_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment. Business justification: Connects quality notifications triggered by shipment issues (e.g., temperature breach) to the specific shipment for corrective actions.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.marketing_brand. Business justification: Quality notifications (holds, deviations, regulatory alerts) in consumer goods are routed to brand managers by brand. Brand-level notification tracking supports brand risk management, campaign pause d']
  nonconformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance. Business justification: SAP QM notifications serve as the formal routing and workflow mechanism for nonconformances. A notification is raised to document and route a nonconformance through the quality system. notification cu']
  product_complaint_id varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this quality notification to an originating customer complaint record, if applicable.']
  submission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.submission. Business justification: Quality notifications flagged as regulatory-reportable (regulatory_reportable_flag, regulatory_report_date, regulatory_authority on notification) are linked to formal regulatory submissions. This FK p']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier associated with the quality issue, used when the notification relates to supplier quality performance.']
  affected_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity of material or product units affected by the quality issue.']
  affected_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the affected quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilograms, L for liters).']
  assigned_to_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person assigned to handle this quality notification.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch or lot number of the affected material, enabling traceability and containment actions.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the quality notification was formally closed after all actions were completed and verified.']
  containment_action varchar [note: 'Immediate containment actions taken to prevent further impact or spread of the quality issue while root cause investigation is underway.']
  containment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when containment actions were implemented.']
  created_date date [note: 'Date when the quality notification was initially created in the system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this quality notification record was first created in the lakehouse, representing the audit trail for data lineage.']
  defect_class varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the defect based on its impact on product safety, functionality, and regulatory compliance.']
  defect_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code classifying the type of defect or quality issue for categorization and trend analysis.']
  detected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the quality issue was first detected or observed, which may differ from when it was formally reported.']
  detection_source varchar [note: 'Origin or source where the quality issue was first identified or detected. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer|production|inspection|audit|supplier|warehouse|field — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  disposition_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the quality authority who approved the material disposition decision.']
  disposition_approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the disposition decision was formally approved.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final disposition decision for the affected material or product: use as-is, rework, scrap, return to supplier, or downgrade to alternate use.']
  financial_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual financial impact of the quality issue including costs of rework, scrap, customer credits, and remediation.']
  financial_impact_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the financial impact amount.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this quality notification record was most recently updated in the lakehouse, supporting change tracking and audit requirements.']
  material_code varchar [note: 'SAP material master code identifying the product or raw material affected by this quality notification.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the material or product affected by the quality issue.']
  notification_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the quality issue, defect, or improvement opportunity documented in this notification.']
  notification_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the quality notification, externally visible and used for tracking and reference across systems.']
  notification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the quality notification indicating its position in the resolution workflow.']
  notification_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the quality notification indicating the nature of the issue: customer complaint, internal defect, improvement request, process deviation, audit finding, or supplier quality issue.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP plant code identifying the manufacturing facility or distribution center responsible for addressing this quality notification.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the notification based on severity, impact, and urgency of resolution required.']
  regulatory_report_date date [note: 'Date when the quality issue was formally reported to the relevant regulatory authority.']
  regulatory_reportable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this quality notification must be reported to regulatory authorities such as FDA, EPA, or CPSC.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the quality issue was formally reported, representing the principal business event timestamp for this notification.']
  responsible_department varchar [note: 'Department or functional area responsible for investigating and resolving the quality notification.']
  root_cause_analysis varchar [note: 'Detailed findings from root cause analysis investigation documenting the underlying cause of the quality issue.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system from which this quality notification record originated (e.g., SAP_QM, VEEVA_VAULT).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this quality notification record in the source system, used for traceability and reconciliation.']
  veeva_vault_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this quality notification to supporting documentation stored in Veeva Vault quality document management system.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method or approach used to verify that corrective actions were effective and the quality issue was resolved.']

  Note: 'SAP QM quality notification records used to formally document and route quality defects, deviations, and improvement requests within the organization. Captures notification type (complaint, defect, improvement), priority, affected material/batch, responsible plant, task assignments, and closure details. Native SAP QM entity.'
}

Table quality.sample {
  sample_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for sample']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_facility. Business justification: DC Sample Collection: samples collected at distribution facilities (environmental monitoring, inbound goods sampling, stability samples) must reference the DC. location_code is a plain text field; str']
  inbound_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.inbound_receipt. Business justification: Inbound Sampling Process: samples drawn from inbound receipts at DCs must reference the receipt for inbound quality traceability. GMP inbound inspection and supplier quality management require linking']
  inspection_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_lot. Business justification: Samples are physically collected as part of an inspection lot — the inspection lot defines the sampling plan, sample size, and collection context. sample currently has no direct FK to inspection_lot; ']
  inventory_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_storage_location. Business justification: Samples are collected from specific warehouse storage locations (environmental monitoring, incoming goods, stability chambers). Linking sample to inventory_storage_location enables location-based qual']
  parent_sample_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on sample (parent_sample_id)']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the finished product from which the sample was taken.']
  analysis_date date [note: 'Date on which the analysis was completed.']
  analysis_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the quality analysis for the sample.']
  analysis_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the analysis result was recorded.']
  analyst_name varchar [note: 'Name of the quality analyst who performed the test.']
  collected_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person who performed the sample collection.']
  collection_date date [note: 'Date on which the sample was physically collected.']
  collection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the sample was collected.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Additional observations or notes recorded by the analyst.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sample record was first created in the system.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the sample is considered expired for testing.']
  is_control boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sample is a control specimen used for method verification.']
  is_retest boolean [note: 'True if the sample is a repeat test due to a prior failure or out‑of‑spec result.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot number associated with the sample.']
  result_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the numeric result.']
  result_value decimal(12,4) [note: 'Quantitative measurement obtained from the analysis (e.g., concentration).']
  sample_code varchar [note: 'External code or barcode assigned to the sample for tracking in the quality system.']
  sample_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name or description of the sample (e.g., "Batch 12 – Tablet Sample").']
  sample_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the sample within the quality process.']
  sample_type varchar [note: 'Category of the sample indicating its purpose in quality testing.']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature at which the sample is stored, expressed in degrees Celsius.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the sample record.']
  volume_ml decimal(10,3) [note: 'Measured volume of the sample in milliliters.']
  weight_g decimal(10,3) [note: 'Measured weight of the sample in grams.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for sample. Referenced by sample_id.'
}

  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the distribution center based on temperature control and operational model. Ambient for room temperature, chilled for refrigerated, frozen for sub-zero, multi-temperature for mixed zones, DSD hub for Direct Store Delivery operations, cross-dock for flow-through distribution.']

  hazmat_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is certified to handle and store hazardous materials. Required for distribution of products with EPA or OSHA regulated substances.']
  inventory_rotation_method varchar [note: 'Primary inventory rotation method used at this facility. FIFO (First In First Out) for standard goods, FEFO (First Expired First Out) for perishables and date-sensitive products, LIFO (Last In First Out) for specific use cases.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the distribution center in decimal degrees. Used for geospatial analytics, route optimization, and logistics planning.']
  lease_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the facility lease agreement. Null for owned facilities. Used for lease renewal planning and facility strategy.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the distribution center in decimal degrees. Used for geospatial analytics, route optimization, and logistics planning.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this distribution center record was last modified. Audit trail for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  opened_date date [note: 'Date when the distribution center commenced operations. Used for facility age analysis and depreciation calculations.']
  operating_hours_weekday varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for weekdays in format HH:MM-HH:MM. Used for scheduling inbound deliveries, outbound shipments, and workforce planning.']
  operating_hours_weekend varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for weekends in format HH:MM-HH:MM. Used for scheduling weekend operations and carrier coordination.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of the distribution center in its lifecycle. Active indicates full operations, inactive for temporarily closed, under construction for new facilities being built, decommissioned for permanently closed, seasonal for facilities operating only during peak periods, maintenance for temporary closure due to upgrades or repairs.']
  osa_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target OSA performance percentage for stores served by this distribution center. OSA measures product availability at retail shelf level.']
  otif_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target OTIF performance percentage for this distribution center. OTIF measures the percentage of orders delivered on time and in full, a key supply chain KPI.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Classification of facility ownership model. Owned for company-owned facilities, leased for long-term leased properties, third-party logistics for 3PL-operated warehouses.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the distribution center address. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.']
  sap_plant_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA plant code representing this distribution center in the ERP system. Maps to the SAP MM and WM modules for material management and warehouse management.']
  sap_storage_location varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA storage location code within the plant. Represents the primary storage location identifier for inventory transactions in SAP WM module.']
  shifts_per_day varchar [note: 'Number of operational shifts per day at the facility. Used for labor planning, throughput capacity modeling, and operational cost analysis.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or regional administrative division where the facility is located. Organizational contact data classified as confidential business information.']
  storage_capacity_pallet_positions varchar [note: 'Maximum number of pallet positions available for storage. Key metric for inventory capacity planning and space utilization analysis.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility has temperature-controlled storage zones. True for facilities with chilled or frozen capabilities, false for ambient-only.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the distribution center location. Used for scheduling, shift planning, and cross-facility coordination.']
  total_capacity_sqft decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total warehouse floor space capacity in square feet. Includes all storage, staging, and operational areas within the facility.']
  wms_site_code varchar [note: 'Unique site identifier in the Blue Yonder WMS system. Used for integration and synchronization between the lakehouse and the operational WMS platform.']

  Note: 'Master record for each physical distribution center (DC) or warehouse facility in the CPG network. Captures DC identity, location, type (ambient, chilled, frozen, DSD hub), capacity metrics, WMS integration identifiers (Blue Yonder site codes), SAP plant/storage location mappings, operating hours, and operational status. SSOT for DC facility master data across the distribution domain.'
}

Table distribution.distribution_storage_location {
  distribution_storage_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for storage_location']
  compliance_obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Storage locations for hazmat, temperature-controlled, or controlled substances must comply with specific regulatory obligations (EPA hazmat storage, FDA cold chain, DEA vault requirements). This link ']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent distribution center facility where this storage location resides.']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'Velocity-based classification of the location for slotting optimization: A (high-velocity/fast-moving), B (medium-velocity), C (low-velocity/slow-moving).']
  aisle varchar [note: 'Aisle designation within the warehouse layout for physical navigation and picking route optimization.']
  bay varchar [note: 'Bay or column position within the aisle for precise horizontal location reference.']
  bin_position varchar [note: 'Specific bin or slot position within the level for granular inventory placement and retrieval.']
  blocked_date date [note: 'Date when the storage location was blocked or made unavailable for operations.']
  blocked_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the location is blocked or unavailable, such as maintenance, damage, safety hold, or quality quarantine.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the storage location record was first created in the WMS system.']
  cycle_count_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency for cycle counting inventory at this location to maintain inventory accuracy and compliance.']
  distribution_storage_location_level varchar [note: 'Vertical level or shelf position within the bay for height-based slotting and equipment compatibility.']
  dsd_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location is designated for DSD operations where products bypass the DC and are delivered directly to retail stores.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the storage location became active and available for warehouse operations.']
  equipment_type_required varchar [note: 'Type of material handling equipment required to access this storage location for put-away and picking operations.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the storage location is scheduled to be decommissioned or removed from active use. Null for indefinite locations.']
  fefo_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location supports FEFO inventory rotation logic for expiry-sensitive products (pharmaceuticals, food, cosmetics).']
  fifo_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location supports FIFO inventory rotation logic for age-based stock management.']
  hazmat_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location is certified and equipped for storing hazardous materials per OSHA and EPA regulations.']
  height_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical height dimension of the storage location in centimeters for vertical clearance and equipment reach validation.']
  last_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent cycle count was performed at this storage location.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the storage location record was most recently updated in the WMS system.']
  length_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical length dimension of the storage location in centimeters for dimensional slotting and equipment compatibility.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the storage location as defined in Blue Yonder WMS. Typically formatted as aisle-bay-level-bin (e.g., A01-B05-L03-P12).']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or description of the storage location for operational reference.']
  location_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the storage location indicating availability for inventory placement and retrieval.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the storage location by operational function: bulk storage, active pick face, reserve inventory, staging area, dock door, or cross-dock zone.']
  mixed_lot_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location permits storage of multiple lot or batch numbers for the same SKU or requires lot segregation.']
  mixed_sku_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location permits storage of multiple SKUs simultaneously or requires single-SKU dedication.']
  pick_face_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this location is designated as an active pick face for order fulfillment operations.']
  picking_strategy varchar [note: 'Order fulfillment picking method supported by this location: batch picking, wave picking, zone picking, discrete (single-order), or cluster picking.']
  putaway_strategy varchar [note: 'Algorithm used to assign incoming inventory to this location: directed (system-assigned), random, fixed (dedicated SKU), or dynamic (velocity-based).']
  replenishment_priority varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for automated replenishment from reserve to pick locations, with lower numbers indicating higher priority.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature control classification for the storage location to ensure product integrity and regulatory compliance (ambient, refrigerated 2-8°C, frozen <-18°C, controlled room temperature).']
  volume_capacity_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum volume capacity of the storage location in cubic meters for space utilization and slotting algorithms.']
  weight_capacity_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum weight capacity of the storage location in kilograms for safe load management and slotting optimization.']
  width_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical width dimension of the storage location in centimeters for dimensional slotting and equipment compatibility.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Logical zone classification within the distribution center for grouping locations by product category, velocity, or operational workflow (e.g., FAST-PICK, SLOW-MOVE, HAZMAT).']

  Note: 'Granular storage location master within a distribution center — aisles, bays, levels, bin positions, and pick faces as defined in Blue Yonder WMS. Tracks location type (bulk, pick, reserve, staging, dock), zone classification, temperature zone, weight/volume capacity, and FEFO/FIFO eligibility flags. Enables put-away and picking optimization at the slot level.'
}

Linking inbound_receipt to supplier_contract']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier or vendor who shipped the goods, representing the counterparty in this receipt transaction.']
  warehouse_distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific warehouse or storage facility within the distribution center where goods were received.']
  accepted_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of goods accepted into inventory after quality inspection, excluding rejected or damaged items.']

  case_count varchar [note: 'Number of cases or cartons received in this inbound shipment.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'ISO standard container identifier for ocean freight shipments, following the BIC (Bureau International des Containers) format.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this receipt record was first created in the system, representing the audit trail start point.']
  discrepancy_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether any discrepancies (quantity, quality, or documentation) were identified during the receiving process.']
  discrepancy_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional details about any discrepancies identified during the receiving process.']
  discrepancy_reason varchar [note: 'Classification of the type of discrepancy identified during receiving, used for root cause analysis and supplier performance management.']
  dock_door_number varchar [note: 'Physical dock door location where the inbound shipment was unloaded, used for labor planning and dock utilization tracking.']
  expected_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity of goods expected to be received based on the ASN or purchase order, used for discrepancy detection.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this receipt record was last updated, supporting audit trail and data lineage requirements.']
  otif_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether this receipt met both on-time delivery and complete quantity requirements, key supplier performance metric.']
  pallet_count varchar [note: 'Number of pallets received in this inbound shipment, used for dock capacity planning and labor allocation.']
  putaway_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when all received goods were moved from the receiving dock to their designated storage locations, completing the inbound process.']
  quality_inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether formal quality inspection was required for this receipt based on product category, supplier risk profile, or regulatory requirements.']
  quality_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the quality inspection process for this receipt, tracking progression through quality control workflow.']
  receipt_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when all receiving activities (unloading, inspection, put-away) were completed and the receipt was closed in the WMS.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the inbound receipt, used for tracking and reference across systems and with suppliers.']
  receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inbound receipt transaction, tracking progression from scheduling through completion or exception handling.']
  receipt_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inbound receipt based on the source and nature of the goods being received.']
  received_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Actual total quantity of goods physically received and counted during the receiving process.']
  rejected_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of goods rejected during receiving due to quality issues, damage, or non-conformance to specifications.']
  scheduled_receipt_date date [note: 'Planned date for the inbound shipment to arrive at the distribution center, based on ASN or supplier commitment.']
  seal_intact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the security seal was found intact upon arrival, critical for quality control and loss prevention.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal identifier applied to the trailer or container, verified upon receipt to ensure shipment integrity and prevent tampering.']
  temperature_check_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether temperature verification was required for this receipt due to cold chain or temperature-sensitive product requirements.']
  temperature_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the recorded temperature was within acceptable range per product specifications and cold chain requirements.']
  temperature_reading_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual temperature reading in Celsius recorded during receipt inspection for temperature-sensitive products, critical for cold chain compliance.']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the trailer or truck that delivered the shipment, used for tracking and carrier performance analysis.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the quantities recorded in this receipt (Each, Case, Pallet, Kilogram, Pound, Liter, Gallon). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|EA|KG|LB|LT|GL — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the physical receipt of goods at a DC from suppliers, manufacturing plants, or inter-DC transfers. Records ASN reference, carrier, trailer/container ID, dock door, receipt date/time, received quantity by SKU/lot, temperature check results, and discrepancy flags. Integrates with SAP WM goods receipt and Blue Yonder WMS inbound processing. Drives inventory on-hand updates and FEFO/FIFO lot registration.'
}

  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Consumer goods companies allocate outbound f']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.promotion_event. Business justification: Promotional order management: outbound orders are directly tied to specific promotion events (e.g., retailer promotional windows). Direct FK enables order-level promotional volume tracking and OTIF-pe']
  freight_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.freight_order. Business justification: Freight order creation for every outbound order manages carrier tendering, freight cost tracking, and audit.']
  marketing_brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand‑level financial and sales reporting aggregates order data by brand, so each outbound order must reference its primary brand.']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating sales order document in the ERP system. Links outbound fulfillment to revenue recognition and customer order management.']
  primary_outbound_distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center fulfilling the outbound order. Determines inventory source and warehouse operations responsible for order execution.']
  product_recall_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_recall. Business justification: During a recall, outbound orders for affected SKUs/lots must be cancelled or placed on hold. This link enables order management to identify and stop shipments of recalled products — a mandatory operat']
  production_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_order. Business justification: Regulatory traceability: outbound orders must reference the production order that generated the shipped product for recall and compliance reporting.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Needed for revenue recognition and profit‑center reporting; each outbound order\'s sales revenue is attributed to a profit center.']
  route_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the delivery route assigned to the outbound order. Used for DSD (Direct Store Delivery) route optimization and multi-stop delivery planning.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.sku.sku_id — Links distribution outbound orders to product master. Required for product-level fulfillment analytics, category-level DC throughput reporting.']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer or retail account placing the outbound order. Links to trade account master for customer details, pricing agreements, and delivery preferences.']
  trade_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to promotion.trade_promotion. Business justification: Trade promotion order fulfillment: outbound orders are generated to fulfill trade promotion volume commitments. Linking enables promotional fill-rate reporting, TPM reconciliation, and OTIF measuremen']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date when the order was actually delivered to the customer destination. Used to calculate OTIF performance and delivery lead time.']
  actual_ship_date date [note: 'Date when the order was actually shipped from the distribution center. Used to measure warehouse execution performance against requested ship date.']
  backorder_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any line items on the order are backordered due to insufficient inventory. True when ordered quantity exceeds available stock.']
  bill_of_lading_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-issued document number serving as receipt of goods and contract of carriage. Required for freight claims and proof of delivery.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description explaining why the order was cancelled. Examples include customer request, inventory shortage, credit hold, or duplicate order. Used for root cause analysis.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the order was cancelled. Null for active orders. Used for cancellation rate analysis and order lifecycle reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the outbound order record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and order lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the order value. Typically USD for domestic US operations, but may vary for export orders.']
  fill_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ordered quantity that was fulfilled from available inventory. Calculated as (shipped quantity / ordered quantity) * 100. Key metric for inventory availability and customer service.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order contains hazardous materials requiring special handling, labeling, and transportation compliance. True for products regulated under DOT hazmat rules.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms defining the division of costs and risks between buyer and seller. Critical for export orders and freight responsibility determination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the outbound order record was last updated. Audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  order_date date [note: 'Date when the outbound order was created or received from the customer. Principal business event timestamp for order lifecycle tracking.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the outbound order. Draft indicates order creation, released means ready for fulfillment, picking/packing/staged represent warehouse execution phases, shipped indicates in-transit, delivered confirms receipt, cancelled indicates order termination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|released|picking|packing|staged|shipped|delivered|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the outbound order based on fulfillment channel and destination. Retail replenishment serves store orders, DSD (Direct Store Delivery) bypasses DC, ecommerce serves online consumers, inter-DC transfer moves inventory between distribution centers, wholesale serves B2B customers, export serves international markets.']
  otif_commitment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this order is subject to formal OTIF performance measurement and customer scorecard reporting. True for orders with contractual delivery commitments.']
  packing_slip_number varchar [note: 'Document number for the packing slip accompanying the shipment. Used for customer receiving and invoice reconciliation.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms agreed with the customer for this order. Examples include Net 30, Net 60, COD (Cash on Delivery), or prepaid. Impacts accounts receivable and cash flow management.']
  pick_ticket_number varchar [note: 'Warehouse document number used to direct picking operations for this order. Generated by WMS when order is released to the warehouse floor.']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the outbound order for warehouse sequencing and resource allocation. Critical orders receive highest priority, rush orders are expedited, expedited orders have faster processing, standard orders follow normal flow.']
  proof_of_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when delivery was confirmed by the recipient. Captured from carrier POD or customer signature. Used for OTIF measurement and freight audit.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Customer\'s purchase order number provided for order tracking and invoice reconciliation. Required for EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) transactions with retail customers.']
  requested_ship_date date [note: 'Date when the customer or system requests the order to be shipped from the distribution center. Used for warehouse planning and scheduling.']
  required_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the order must be delivered to the customer destination. Critical for OTIF (On Time In Full) performance measurement and SLA compliance.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'Delivery speed commitment for the outbound order. Determines carrier selection, freight cost, and OTIF measurement criteria.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Transportation mode used for order delivery. Ground for truck, air for expedited freight, ocean for international, rail for bulk, parcel for small packages, LTL (Less Than Truckload) for partial loads, FTL (Full Truckload) for full loads. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ground|air|ocean|rail|parcel|ltl|ftl — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text instructions for warehouse and logistics teams regarding special handling requirements. May include temperature control, fragile handling, hazmat procedures, or customer-specific delivery instructions.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order requires temperature-controlled storage and transportation. True for cold chain products requiring refrigeration or freezing.']
  total_order_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity of units across all line items in the outbound order. Used for warehouse capacity planning and OTIF fill rate calculation.']
  total_order_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the outbound order before taxes and freight charges. Used for order prioritization, credit limit checks, and revenue forecasting.']
  total_order_volume_m3 decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total volume of the outbound order in cubic meters. Used for warehouse space planning, truck cube utilization, and pallet configuration.']
  total_order_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total weight of the outbound order in kilograms. Used for freight cost calculation, carrier capacity planning, and vehicle loading optimization.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking number for shipment visibility and customer self-service tracking. Used for parcel and LTL shipments.']
  wave_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse wave that includes this order. Wave picking groups orders for efficient batch picking and resource optimization.']

  Note: 'Master outbound fulfillment order record representing a customer or retailer replenishment request to be fulfilled from a DC. Captures order number, order type (retail replenishment, DSD, e-commerce, inter-DC transfer), customer/account reference, requested ship date, required delivery date, priority, OTIF commitment, and order status lifecycle. Sourced from SAP SD and Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud order management.'
}

Table distribution.pick_task {
  pick_task_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the pick task record. Primary key for the pick_task data product.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Pick task labor costs are charged to a cost center; required for labor cost analysis and OTIF performance metrics.']
  destination_location_distribution_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the target location where picked items are staged or packed (e.g., pack station, staging lane, loading dock).']
  distribution_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center or warehouse where the pick task is executed.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific lot or batch of inventory being picked. Critical for FEFO (First Expired First Out) and FIFO (First In First Out) rotation compliance.']
  outbound_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent outbound order or shipment order that generated this pick task.']
  primary_pick_distribution_storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse storage location (bin, shelf, pallet position) from which inventory is picked.']
  product_recall_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to regulatory.product_recall. Business justification: During an active recall, pick tasks for affected SKUs/lots must be immediately suspended in the WMS. This link enables real-time halting of picking operations for recalled inventory — a critical opera']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product SKU being picked or packed in this task.']
  carton_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the carton or shipping container into which items are packed. May be a license plate number (LPN) or SSCC (Serial Shipping Container Code).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pick task record was first created in the WMS. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  dsd_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this pick task is part of a Direct Store Delivery (DSD) execution workflow (true = DSD, false = standard distribution).']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying any exception or issue encountered during task execution (e.g., short pick, damaged goods, location discrepancy). Null if no exception.']
  exception_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the operator or supervisor describing the exception or issue encountered during task execution.']
