Examples: SAP_FI (SAP S/4HANA Financial Accounting), SAP_CO (SAP Controlling), ORACLE_GL (Oracle Cloud General Ledger), ORACLE_AP (Oracle Accounts Payable), ORACLE_AR (Oracle Accounts Receivable), SFDC (Salesforce), WMS (Warehouse Management System), MES (Manufacturing Execution System). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: SAP_FI|SAP_CO|ORACLE_GL|ORACLE_AP|ORACLE_AR|SFDC|WMS|MES — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sox_control_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry is subject to SOX internal control testing and audit procedures. True if SOX-controlled, False otherwise.']
  tax_reporting_date date [note: 'The date relevant for tax reporting purposes. May differ from posting date based on tax jurisdiction rules. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  trading_partner_company_code varchar [note: 'Company code of the intercompany trading partner if this is an intercompany transaction. Used for consolidation and elimination entries. Null if not an intercompany transaction.']
  transaction_code varchar [note: 'SAP transaction code or Oracle program identifier used to create this journal entry. Examples: FB01 (Post Document), FB50 (G/L Account Posting), MIRO (Enter Invoice), F-28 (Post Incoming Payments). Used for audit trail and process analysis.']

  Note: 'Core general ledger journal entry header capturing every financial posting in the consumer goods enterprise. Records document type (vendor invoice, customer payment, goods issue, payroll posting, accrual, reversal), posting date, fiscal period, company code, ledger, currency, exchange rate, reference document, and SOX-compliant posting user and approval chain. The authoritative transactional record for all financial activity sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI and Oracle Cloud Financials.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry_line {
  journal_entry_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the journal entry line item. Primary key for granular cost allocation, COGS decomposition, and SOX-compliant audit trail tracing at the line level.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: In SAP FI, journal entry line items can carry their own company code (especially in intercompany scenarios where lines post to different entities). Normalizing the existing company_code STRING to a FK']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Journal entry line items carry cost center assignments for CO cost allocation. The existing cost_center_code STRING column should be normalized to a proper FK referencing the cost_center master table.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Journal entry lines store GL account code; FK to gl_account enables proper account lookup and eliminates duplicate code.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent journal entry header document. Links this line to the overall journal entry batch and posting context.']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.material. Business justification: Journal entry lines for inventory movements, GR/IR, and COGS postings reference material masters in consumer goods ERP. journal_entry_line.material_number is a denormalized signal. This FK enables mat']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Journal entry line items carry profit center assignments for segment reporting. The existing profit_center_code STRING column should be normalized to a proper FK referencing the profit_center master t']
  stock_movement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_movement. Business justification: Every goods movement (GR, GI, transfer posting) in Consumer Goods generates journal entry lines for inventory valuation. Linking journal_entry_line to stock_movement enables inventory-to-GL reconcilia']
  amount_company_code_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount converted to the local currency of the company code. Used for statutory reporting and local GAAP compliance.']
  amount_group_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount converted to the group reporting currency. Enables consolidated financial reporting across all subsidiaries.']
  amount_transaction_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of this line item in the original transaction currency. Preserves the original posting amount before currency conversion.']
  assignment_field varchar [note: 'Free-text assignment field for additional reference information such as invoice number, purchase order, or payment reference. Enhances traceability.']
  baseline_payment_date date [note: 'Baseline date for payment terms calculation. Used to compute due dates and cash discount periods.']
  business_area_code varchar [note: 'Business area classification for cross-company code segment reporting. Enables consolidated financial statements by business segment.']
  clearing_date date [note: 'Date on which this open item was cleared or settled. Null if the item remains open.']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the clearing entry that settled this open item. Null if the item remains open.']
  company_code_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the company code local currency. Typically the statutory reporting currency of the legal entity.']
  customer_account_number varchar [note: 'Customer account number for revenue and receivables postings. Links financial transactions to customer master data.']
  debit_credit_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this line is a debit (D) or credit (C) posting. Fundamental to double-entry bookkeeping and account balance calculation.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is due for payables or expected for receivables. Critical for DSO and cash flow management.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate applied to convert transaction currency to company code or group currency. Critical for currency translation audit trail.']
  functional_area_code varchar [note: 'Functional area classification (e.g., production, sales, administration, R&D). Supports cost-of-sales accounting and functional expense reporting.']
  group_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the group reporting currency. Typically USD or EUR for multinational consumer goods companies.']
  internal_order_number varchar [note: 'Internal order for tracking costs of short-term jobs, events, or overhead activities. Enables job costing and overhead allocation.']
  line_item_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the journal entry header. Determines the ordering and position of this line in the document.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Payment terms applicable to this line item. Determines due date calculation and cash discount eligibility.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing plant or distribution center associated with this line item. Supports plant-level cost accounting and inventory management.']
  posting_key varchar [note: 'Two-digit posting key controlling account type, debit/credit indicator, and field status. Fundamental to SAP FI posting logic.']
  quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of material or units associated with this line item. Enables unit cost calculation and inventory valuation.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External or internal document number referenced by this line (e.g., invoice, purchase order, contract). Supports document linkage and reconciliation.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this line item is a reversal of a previous posting. True if this is a reversal entry.']
  reversed_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the original entry that this line reverses. Null if this is not a reversal.']
  special_gl_indicator varchar [note: 'Special GL indicator for down payments, bills of exchange, guarantees, or other special transactions. Enables special ledger processing.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'Storage location within the plant for inventory-related postings. Enables warehouse-level inventory tracking and valuation.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax amount calculated for this line item in transaction currency. Supports VAT, sales tax, and other indirect tax reporting.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code determining the tax treatment of this line item. Drives automatic tax calculation and tax reporting compliance.']
  text_description varchar [note: 'Descriptive text explaining the nature or purpose of this journal entry line. Provides human-readable context for audit and review.']
  trading_partner_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the intercompany trading partner for intercompany transactions. Enables intercompany reconciliation and elimination entries.']
  transaction_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the transaction amount. Identifies the currency in which the original transaction was denominated.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity field (e.g., EA, KG, LT). Standardizes quantity representation across transactions.']
  value_date date [note: 'Value date for cash management and bank reconciliation. Represents the effective date for interest calculation and cash position.']
  vendor_account_number varchar [note: 'Vendor account number for procurement and payables postings. Links financial transactions to vendor master data.']
  wbs_element_code varchar [note: 'WBS element for project-related postings. Links costs and revenues to specific projects, phases, or deliverables in project accounting.']

  Note: 'Individual line items of a general ledger journal entry. Each line captures the GL account, cost center, profit center, WBS element, business area, debit/credit indicator, amount in transaction currency, amount in company code currency, amount in group currency, tax code, and assignment field. Enables granular cost allocation, COGS decomposition, and audit trail tracing at the line level as required by SOX compliance.'
}

Table finance.ar_invoice {
  ar_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the accounts receivable invoice record. Primary key for the AR invoice entity.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: AR invoices are issued by a specific legal entity (company code). Normalizing the existing company_code STRING to a FK ensures referential integrity with the company_code master.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: AR invoices reference GL accounts by code; adding FK normalizes accounting data.']
  sales_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.invoice. Business justification: AR invoice reconciliation process matches each AR invoice to the originating sales invoice for cash application.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: AR invoices originate from sales orders; direct FK enables order-to-cash reconciliation and DSO analysis by order. Consumer goods AR teams trace deductions and disputes back to originating orders. sal']
  trade_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the trade account (customer) to whom this invoice was issued. Links to the customer master record.']
  amount_received decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount received from the customer against this invoice. May be less than total amount due to partial payments or deductions. Null for unpaid invoices.']
  billing_date date [note: 'Date when the invoice was created and issued to the customer. Principal business event timestamp for the invoice lifecycle.']
  billing_document_type varchar [note: 'SAP billing document type code indicating the nature of the invoice (e.g., F2=standard invoice, G2=credit memo, L2=debit memo). Determines accounting treatment and revenue recognition.']
  clearing_date date [note: 'Date when the invoice was cleared in the financial system, either through full payment or write-off. Null for open invoices.']
  clearing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number assigned when the invoice is cleared in the financial system. Links to the clearing document for audit trail. Null for open invoices.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the invoice record was first created in the AR system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the invoice is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Critical for multi-currency reporting and foreign exchange management.']
  deduction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of deductions taken by the customer against this invoice. Includes short-payments, chargebacks, and unauthorized deductions requiring resolution.']
  deduction_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for customer deductions (e.g., pricing dispute, damaged goods, short shipment, promotional allowance). Used for deduction root cause analysis.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice is currently under dispute by the customer. True if disputed, false otherwise. Disputed invoices are excluded from standard collection processes.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason the customer is disputing the invoice. Used for dispute resolution and root cause analysis.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the goods were sold (e.g., retail, wholesale, DTC, e-commerce). Used for channel profitability analysis.']
  dso_aging_bucket varchar [note: 'Aging category based on number of days the invoice has been outstanding past the due date. Used for DSO reporting, collections prioritization, and bad debt provisioning.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment is expected from the customer based on agreed payment terms. Used for Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) calculation and dunning.']
  dunning_level varchar [note: 'Current dunning level indicating the escalation stage of collection efforts (0=no dunning, 1=first reminder, 2=second reminder, etc.). Higher levels indicate more aggressive collection actions.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year when the invoice was posted. Used for monthly financial reporting and trend analysis.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the invoice was posted. Used for year-over-year financial analysis and annual reporting.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'Date when the invoice was posted to the general ledger. Used for financial period assignment and month-end close processes.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total invoice amount before any trade discounts, deductions, or adjustments. Represents the full billed value of goods or services.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique invoice number assigned by the billing system. Used for customer communication and payment reference.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the invoice in the accounts receivable process. Tracks progression from open through payment to clearing or write-off. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|partially_paid|paid|cleared|written_off|disputed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_dunning_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent dunning notice was sent to the customer. Used to track collection activity frequency and determine next dunning action.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who last modified the invoice record. Required for SOX compliance and segregation of duties controls.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the invoice record was last modified. Used for change tracking and SOX compliance audit trails.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Invoice amount after trade discounts but before tax. This is the base amount subject to taxation and the primary value for revenue recognition.']
  outstanding_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining unpaid balance on the invoice. Calculated as total amount minus amount received. Zero when invoice is fully paid or cleared.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date when payment was received from the customer. Null for unpaid invoices. Used for DSO calculation and cash flow analysis.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the customer is expected to remit payment. Used for cash application automation and payment reconciliation.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the payment terms agreed with the customer (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30). Determines due date calculation and early payment discount eligibility.']
  revenue_recognition_date date [note: 'Date when revenue from this invoice is recognized in the financial statements under ASC 606/IFRS 15. May differ from billing date based on performance obligation fulfillment.']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'SAP sales organization responsible for the sale. Used for sales performance reporting and commission calculation.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount charged on the invoice, including sales tax, VAT, GST, or other applicable indirect taxes based on jurisdiction.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final invoice amount including all discounts and taxes. This is the total amount due from the customer and the target for cash application.']
  trade_discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total trade discounts applied to the invoice, including volume discounts, promotional allowances, and negotiated rebates. Reduces gross amount to net amount.']
  write_off_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount written off as uncollectible bad debt. Null for invoices not written off. Impacts bad debt expense and allowance for doubtful accounts.']
  write_off_date date [note: 'Date when the invoice or portion thereof was written off as uncollectible. Null for invoices not written off.']
  write_off_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for write-off (e.g., customer bankruptcy, uncollectible, small balance write-off). Used for bad debt analysis and credit policy refinement.']

  Note: 'Accounts receivable master record representing the full trade receivable lifecycle for consumer goods sales to retailers, distributors, and wholesalers. Owns invoice creation, payment terms, cash receipt application, partial payments, deductions/short-payments, clearing, and write-offs. Captures invoice number, billing date, due date, payment terms, gross amount, trade discounts, net amount, tax, currency, payment method, payment date, amount received, clearing reference, DSO aging bucket, dunning level, and deduction reason codes. The single source of truth for all receivable balances, cash application, deduction management, DSO tracking, cash flow forecasting, and revenue recognition under ASC 606/IFRS 15. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA SD billing and FI-AR.'
}

The existing bank_account_code BIGINT column is already the correct data type and semantically maps to bank_account.bank_account_id. Normalizing']

  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: AR payments are attributed to cost centers for revenue and cash allocation in SAP CO. The existing cost_center_code STRING column should be normalized to a proper FK referencing the cost_center master']

  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year when payment was posted. Used for period-based financial reporting and year-over-year analysis.']
  local_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Payment amount converted to company local currency using the exchange rate. Used for consolidated financial reporting.']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter code for the company local currency. Used for financial reporting and consolidation.']
  lockbox_batch_number varchar [note: 'Lockbox batch identifier when payment received through bank lockbox service. Used for lockbox reconciliation.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person or system that last modified the payment record. Used for SOX compliance and audit trail.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  overpayment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether payment amount exceeds the invoice balance. True if customer paid more than owed.']
  partial_payment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a partial payment against the invoice. True if payment amount is less than invoice balance.']
  payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount received in the payment transaction, in the payment currency. Gross payment before any deductions or adjustments.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which payment was received. Distinguishes interface from payment instrument.']
  payment_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the payment amount. Identifies the currency in which payment was received.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date when the payment was received or posted. Used for Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) calculation and cash flow analysis.']
  payment_document_number varchar [note: 'External payment document number or reference provided by the customer or payment processor. Used for reconciliation and audit trail.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method or instrument used for payment. Includes ACH (Automated Clearing House), wire transfer, check, credit card, debit card, or EFT.']
  payment_processor_code varchar [note: 'Transaction identifier from third-party payment processor (e.g., Stripe, PayPal, Adyen). Used for processor reconciliation.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the payment in the cash application workflow. Tracks payment lifecycle from receipt through clearing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: received|cleared|applied|partially_applied|unapplied|reversed|rejected — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Payment terms code from the original invoice (e.g., NET30, 2/10NET30). Used to validate discount eligibility and calculate DSO.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date when the payment was posted to the general ledger. Used for financial period assignment and reporting.']
  remittance_advice_number varchar [note: 'Reference number from customer remittance advice document. Links payment to customer payment notification (EDI 820 or paper remittance).']
  reversal_date date [note: 'Date when payment was reversed. Null if payment has not been reversed.']
  reversal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment has been reversed. True if payment was subsequently voided or returned.']
  reversal_reason_code varchar [note: 'Reason code for payment reversal. Includes NSF (non-sufficient funds), stop payment, duplicate, fraud, customer request, or system error. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NSF|stop_payment|duplicate|fraud|customer_request|bank_error|system_error — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  short_payment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customer paid less than invoice amount without authorized deduction. Triggers deduction management workflow.']
  value_date date [note: 'Date when funds become available in the bank account. May differ from payment date due to clearing time.']

  Note: 'Accounts receivable payment record capturing cash receipts from trade customers against outstanding AR invoices. Records payment date, payment method (ACH, wire, check, EDI 820), amount received, currency, exchange rate, bank account, clearing document reference, partial payment flag, and deduction/short-payment details. Supports cash application, deduction management, and DSO calculation. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI-AR and Oracle Cloud Receivables.'
}

Table finance.ap_invoice {
  ap_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the accounts payable invoice record. Primary key for the AP invoice entity.']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the company bank account from which payment was or will be made. Used for cash management and bank reconciliation.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: AP invoices are processed under a specific company code in SAP FI. Normalizing the existing company_code STRING to a FK ensures referential integrity with the company_code master.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: AP invoices are attributed to cost centers for expense allocation in SAP CO. The existing cost_center_code STRING column should be normalized to a proper FK referencing the cost_center master table.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: AP invoices capture GL account code; FK to gl_account creates a true parent‑child relationship and removes the code column.']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the goods receipt document used in three-way match verification to confirm materials or services were received.']
  inbound_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to distribution.inbound_receipt. Business justification: AP invoices are matched against inbound receipts in the 3-way match process (PO + goods receipt + invoice). Consumer goods AP teams require this link to validate supplier invoices against DC receipts,']
  po_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line. Business justification: Line-level 3-way match in Consumer Goods AP requires matching AP invoice lines to specific PO lines. Current ap_invoice only links at PO header level. Line-level matching is standard for quantity/pric']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: AP invoices are attributed to profit centers for cost allocation and P&L reporting. The existing profit_center_code STRING column should be normalized to a proper FK referencing the profit_center mast']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order against which this invoice is matched. Used for three-way match verification.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor or supplier who issued this invoice. Links to the vendor master record.']
  supplier_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_invoice. Business justification: 3-way match reconciliation (PO/GR/Invoice) in Consumer Goods AP processing requires finance ap_invoice to reference the procurement-side supplier_invoice record. Enables payment reconciliation, disput']
  baseline_date date [note: 'The reference date from which payment terms are calculated. Typically the invoice date or goods receipt date depending on payment term configuration.']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'The financial document number that cleared the open payable balance. Links the invoice to the payment transaction for reconciliation.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID of the person who created the invoice record. Required for SOX-compliant audit trails and segregation of duties verification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the invoice record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and Sarbanes-Oxley (SOX) compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency in which the invoice is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cash discount amount available if payment is made within the early payment discount period. Used for working capital optimization.']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which payment must be made to the vendor according to the payment terms. Used for cash flow planning and Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) tracking.']
  early_payment_discount_taken decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual cash discount amount captured by paying the invoice within the early payment discount period. Key metric for working capital optimization.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,5) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate used to convert the invoice amount to the company\'s local currency for financial reporting.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year in which the invoice was posted. Used for monthly financial reporting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the invoice was posted. Used for period-based financial reporting and year-end closing.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total invoice amount before any deductions, including all line items, taxes, and freight charges.']
  invoice_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice by spend category (e.g., raw materials, packaging, contract manufacturing, indirect goods, services). Used for spend analytics and procurement reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: raw_materials|packaging|contract_manufacturing|indirect_goods|services|freight|utilities|rent — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  invoice_date date [note: 'The date the vendor issued the invoice. This is the document date from the vendor\'s invoice and is used for aging analysis.']
  invoice_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description or header text providing additional context about the invoice, such as the nature of goods or services purchased.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the invoice in the accounts payable process. Tracks the invoice from receipt through payment and clearing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|posted|approved|paid|partially_paid|cancelled|on_hold — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  match_status varchar [note: 'Status of the three-way match verification process comparing the invoice to the purchase order and goods receipt. Critical for payment approval workflow.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID of the person who last modified the invoice record. Required for SOX-compliant audit trails.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the invoice record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net payable amount after deducting any discounts or credits. This is the amount that will be paid to the vendor.']
  payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual amount paid to the vendor. May differ from net amount if partial payments or adjustments were made.']
  payment_block_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating if the invoice is blocked for payment and the reason for the block (e.g., price variance, quantity variance, quality issue, pending approval).']
  payment_date date [note: 'The actual date payment was executed to the vendor. Used for cash flow tracking and Days Inventory Outstanding (DIO) calculation.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The method by which payment will be or was made to the vendor (e.g., wire transfer, ACH, check, EFT, credit card, virtual card).']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the payment terms agreed with the vendor (e.g., Net 30, 2/10 Net 30). Defines the due date calculation and early payment discount eligibility.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date the invoice was posted to the general ledger. This date determines the fiscal period for financial reporting.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'Additional reference number for cross-referencing to other documents such as delivery notes, packing slips, or contract numbers.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total tax amount charged on the invoice, including sales tax, VAT, GST, or other applicable taxes.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The tax jurisdiction code or tax type applied to the invoice (e.g., sales tax, VAT, GST). Used for tax reporting and compliance.']
  vendor_bank_account_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor\'s bank account to which payment was or will be sent. Required for electronic payment processing.']
  vendor_invoice_number varchar [note: 'The invoice number assigned by the vendor. This is the external reference number printed on the vendor\'s invoice document.']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of withholding tax deducted from the payment to the vendor as required by tax regulations. Must be remitted to tax authorities.']

  Note: 'Accounts payable master record representing the full trade payable lifecycle for raw materials, packaging, contract manufacturing, and indirect goods/services. Owns invoice receipt, three-way match verification, payment scheduling, payment execution, early payment discount capture, and clearing. Captures vendor invoice number, invoice date, posting date, due date, gross amount, tax, net amount, payment terms, currency, match status, payment method, payment date, payment amount, payment run ID, bank account reference, early discount taken, clearing document, and payment block details. The single source of truth for all payable balances, payment execution, DIO tracking, working capital optimization, and supplier payment management. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA MM-IV, FI-AP, and Oracle Cloud Payables.'
}

Table finance.ap_payment {
  ap_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the accounts payable payment record. Primary key.']
  ap_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ap_invoice. Business justification: AP payments settle AP invoices — this is the core accounts payable payment application relationship. ap_payment currently has invoice_id pointing to sales.invoice (cross-domain, which appears semantic']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.bank_account. Business justification: AP payments are disbursed from a specific bank account. The existing bank_account_code BIGINT column is already the correct data type and semantically maps to bank_account.bank_account_id. Normalizing']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: AP payments are executed from a specific company code. Normalizing the existing company_code STRING to a FK ensures referential integrity with the company_code master.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: AP payments are attributed to cost centers for cash flow and expense tracking in SAP CO. The existing cost_center_code STRING column should be normalized to a proper FK referencing the cost_center mas']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: AP payments include GL account code; FK to gl_account provides proper accounting linkage.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the invoice being paid. Links to the accounts payable invoice record.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: AP payments are attributed to profit centers for cash flow and P&L reporting. The existing profit_center_code STRING column should be normalized to a proper FK referencing the profit_center master tab']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier receiving the payment. Links to the supplier master data.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who approved the payment. Supports SOX-compliant audit trails and segregation of duties controls.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment was approved. Supports audit trails and payment cycle time analysis.']
  clearing_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment cleared the open invoice in the accounts payable subledger. Used for Days Inventory Outstanding (DIO) and working capital analysis.']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'The document number assigned when the payment cleared the open invoice in the accounts payable subledger. Used for reconciliation and audit trails.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the payment record was first created in the system. Supports audit trails and data lineage tracking.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The early payment discount amount taken by the company for paying the invoice before the discount deadline. Supports working capital optimization and supplier relationship management.']
  discount_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the payment must be made to qualify for the early payment discount. Used for working capital optimization decisions.']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which the payment was contractually due to the supplier. Used to calculate on-time payment performance and avoid late payment penalties.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'The exchange rate applied to convert the payment amount from the payment currency to the company\'s local currency. Used for financial consolidation and reporting.']
  local_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The payment amount converted to the company\'s local reporting currency using the exchange rate. Used for consolidated financial reporting and EBITDA calculations.']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the company\'s local reporting currency (e.g., USD). Used for financial consolidation.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified the payment record. Supports SOX-compliant audit trails and change tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the payment record was last modified. Supports audit trails and change tracking for SOX compliance.']
  net_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The net payment amount after applying early payment discounts and any other adjustments. This is the actual amount transferred to the supplier.']
  payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The gross payment amount remitted to the supplier in the payment currency, before any adjustments or discounts.']
  payment_approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the payment. Pending indicates awaiting approval, approved indicates authorized for processing, rejected indicates payment was denied. Supports SOX-compliant approval workflows.']
  payment_block_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating if the payment was blocked for review or approval. Blank indicates no block. Used for payment control and fraud prevention.']
  payment_block_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason the payment was blocked (e.g., pending invoice verification, supplier on hold, duplicate payment suspected). Used for exception management.']
  payment_block_released_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who released the payment block and authorized the payment to proceed. Supports SOX-compliant audit trails.']
  payment_block_released_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment block was released and the payment was authorized to proceed. Supports audit trails and payment cycle time analysis.']
  payment_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency payment processing.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date on which the payment was issued or processed. Used for cash flow reporting and Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) calculations.']
  payment_document_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known payment document number assigned by the ERP system. Used for reconciliation and audit trails.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment instrument used to remit funds to the supplier. ACH (Automated Clearing House), wire transfer, check, EDI 820 (Electronic Data Interchange payment order), credit card, or direct debit.']
  payment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the payment. Used to capture special instructions, exceptions, or additional context for audit and reconciliation purposes.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number provided to the supplier for payment identification and reconciliation (e.g., check number, wire confirmation number, ACH trace number).']
  payment_run_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payment run batch that grouped multiple payments for processing. Used for batch reconciliation and audit trails.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment. Draft indicates initial creation, pending awaits approval, approved is ready for processing, processed has been sent to bank, cleared has been confirmed by bank, failed indicates processing error, cancelled indicates payment was voided. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending|approved|processed|cleared|failed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The payment terms agreed with the supplier (e.g., Net 30, 2/10 Net 30). Defines the due date and early payment discount eligibility.']
  remittance_advice_sent_date date [note: 'The date on which the remittance advice was sent to the supplier. Used for supplier communication tracking.']
  remittance_advice_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a remittance advice was sent to the supplier to notify them of the payment details. True indicates sent, False indicates not sent.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created the payment record. Supports SOX-compliant audit trails.']

  Note: 'Accounts payable payment record capturing outgoing payments to suppliers for raw materials, packaging, and services. Records payment date, payment method (ACH, wire, check, EDI 820), payment amount, currency, exchange rate, bank account, payment run ID, clearing document, early payment discount taken, and payment block release authorization. Supports cash flow management, supplier relationship management, and working capital optimization. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI-AP.'
}

Table finance.cogs_allocation {
  cogs_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the COGS allocation record. Primary key for the product costing and COGS allocation master.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center responsible for the production activity. Enables cost center profitability analysis.']
  hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.hierarchy. Business justification: COGS allocation by product hierarchy supports category and division P&L reporting in consumer goods. This FK enables hierarchy-level COGS roll-up, category margin analysis, and IBP-to-finance reconcil']
  manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant where the product is produced and costed.']
  product_bom_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the bill of materials used for this cost calculation. Links cost to recipe/formula structure.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the profit center for internal P&L reporting and segment profitability analysis.']
  routing_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production routing (sequence of operations) used for this cost calculation. Links cost to manufacturing process.']
  sales_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sales.invoice. Business justification: COGS is recognized when revenue is recognized at invoice. Consumer goods gross margin reporting requires linking COGS allocations to sales invoices for product-level profitability analysis, trade prom']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material (finished good, semi-finished good, or raw material) for which COGS is allocated.']
  standard_cost_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.standard_cost. Business justification: COGS allocation records reference the standard cost estimate used as the basis for cost allocation. In CPG manufacturing, COGS allocations are derived from standard cost estimates (SAP CK11N). This in']
  allocation_date date [note: 'Date when the COGS allocation was calculated and posted. Key business event timestamp for cost assignment.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to allocate costs to the product: direct (traced), activity_based (ABC costing), weighted_average, FIFO (first-in-first-out), or standard (predetermined rate).']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who approved and released this cost estimate for use in inventory valuation and margin analysis.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cost estimate was approved and released. Critical for SOX compliance and audit trail.']
  costing_lot_size decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity basis used for cost calculation. Fixed costs are spread over this lot size to determine unit cost.']
  costing_sheet varchar [note: 'Template defining overhead calculation rules and cost component structure for this allocation.']
  costing_type varchar [note: 'Type of cost estimate: standard (baseline for valuation), actual (realized costs), planned (budgeted), or simulated (what-if scenario).']
  costing_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the costing run (e.g., standard cost, planned cost, actual cost). Enables parallel costing scenarios and version comparison.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this COGS allocation record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all cost amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  depreciation_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Allocated depreciation expense for production equipment and facilities used in manufacturing.']
  direct_labor_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cost component for direct production labor involved in manufacturing the product.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year for the COGS allocation. Supports monthly cost tracking and variance analysis.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year for which the COGS allocation applies. Enables year-over-year cost trend analysis.']
  fixed_overhead_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fixed manufacturing overhead costs that remain constant regardless of production volume (e.g., facility rent, salaries).']
  freight_in_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Inbound freight and logistics costs for raw materials and components. Part of landed cost calculation.']
  lot_size_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the costing lot size (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for liter).']
  machine_overhead_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cost component for machine and equipment usage, including depreciation, maintenance, and operating costs.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this COGS allocation record was last modified. Audit trail for record updates.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments regarding this cost allocation, including assumptions, adjustments, or special circumstances.']
  packaging_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cost component for primary, secondary, and tertiary packaging materials. Critical for CPG product costing.']
  price_control_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether material is valued at standard price (S) or moving average price (V). Critical for inventory valuation method.']
  raw_material_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cost component for raw materials and ingredients used in production. Key driver of COGS in consumer goods manufacturing.']
  release_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the cost estimate: draft (in progress), released (approved for use), locked (frozen for audit), archived (historical).']
  total_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total allocated cost per unit including all cost components (raw material, packaging, labor, overhead, freight, depreciation).']
  validity_end_date date [note: 'End date until which this cost estimate remains valid. Null indicates open-ended validity.']
  validity_start_date date [note: 'Start date from which this cost estimate is valid and applicable for inventory valuation and margin calculation.']
  valuation_class varchar [note: 'Classification code that determines which general ledger accounts are updated during inventory movements and COGS posting.']
  variable_overhead_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Variable manufacturing overhead costs that fluctuate with production volume (e.g., utilities, indirect materials).']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between standard cost and actual cost. Positive indicates unfavorable variance (actual > standard), negative indicates favorable variance.']
  variance_category varchar [note: 'Classification of cost variance by root cause: price (input cost change), quantity (usage deviation), mix (recipe change), yield (output efficiency), efficiency (labor/machine productivity), volume (fixed cost absorption).']

  Note: 'Product costing and COGS allocation master for CPG manufacturing. Owns both standard cost estimates (planned) and actual cost allocations for finished goods, semi-finished goods, and raw materials. Captures cost component splits (raw material, packaging, direct labor, variable/fixed overhead, machine overhead, freight-in, depreciation), costing version, validity period, costing lot size, release status, plant, allocation date, fiscal period, standard vs actual cost, variance amount, variance category, and allocation method. The single source of truth for inventory valuation, SKU-level and brand-level gross margin analysis, manufacturing variance reporting, COGS decomposition, and make-vs-buy cost comparison. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA CO-PC (Product Costing).'
}

Table finance.standard_cost {
  standard_cost_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the standard cost record. Primary key for the standard cost master data.']
  approved_supplier_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.approved_supplier_list. Business justification: Standard cost setting in Consumer Goods uses ASL pricing (price_validity dates, quality ratings) as the basis for raw material cost estimates. Links the cost estimate to the specific approved supplier']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Standard cost records are defined per cost center; linking enables cost‑center level consolidation and provides a proper relational anchor.']
  primary_standard_manufacturing_facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing plant or production facility where this standard cost applies. Standard costs are plant-specific in CPG manufacturing.']
  primary_standard_sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material (finished good, semi-finished good, or raw material) for which this standard cost is defined. Links to the product master.']
  product_bom_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_bom. Business justification: Standard cost estimates in consumer goods are built from specific BOM versions. standard_cost.bom_usage (plain attr) signals this dependency. This FK enables cost roll-up traceability from standard co']
  supplier_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_contract. Business justification: In Consumer Goods, standard cost estimates for raw materials and packaging are set using contracted supplier prices. Cost accountants reference the active supplier contract during annual standard cost']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval workflow status for this standard cost estimate. Ensures proper governance and SOX compliance for cost changes.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the manager or controller who approved this standard cost estimate. Required for SOX compliance and audit trail.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this standard cost estimate was approved. Critical for audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  bom_usage varchar [note: 'The BOM usage type (e.g., production, costing, engineering) that was used as the basis for material cost calculation in this estimate.']
  cost_estimate_created_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the cost accountant or system user who created this cost estimate. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  cost_estimate_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this standard cost record was first created in the source system. Critical for audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  cost_estimate_modified_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the cost accountant or system user who last modified this cost estimate. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  cost_estimate_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this standard cost record was last modified in the source system. Critical for audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  cost_estimate_number varchar [note: 'The unique business identifier for the cost estimate document in the source ERP system. Used for audit trail and traceability.']
  cost_variance_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The absolute difference between the current standard cost and the previous standard cost. Positive indicates cost increase, negative indicates cost decrease.']
  cost_variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage change in standard cost compared to the previous period. Calculated as (current - previous) / previous * 100.']
  costing_lot_size decimal(18,3) [note: 'The production lot size (quantity) used as the basis for calculating the standard cost. Fixed costs are spread over this quantity.']
  costing_lot_size_uom varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the costing lot size (e.g., EA, KG, L, CS). Must align with the materials base unit of measure.']
  costing_sheet varchar [note: 'The costing sheet identifier that defines the overhead calculation rules and surcharge rates applied in this cost estimate.']
  costing_type varchar [note: 'The type of cost estimate: standard (for inventory valuation), planned (for budgeting), actual (for variance analysis), or simulated (for what-if scenarios).']
  costing_version varchar [note: 'The costing version identifier that groups standard cost estimates for a specific planning or valuation scenario (e.g., current year, budget, simulation). Enables parallel costing scenarios.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the standard cost is expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  direct_labor_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The cost component for direct production labor (machine operators, line workers) allocated to one unit based on standard labor hours and rates.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year for which this standard cost estimate is applicable. Enables period-specific cost tracking.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year for which this standard cost estimate is applicable. Standard costs are typically set annually and used for budgeting and variance analysis.']
  fixed_overhead_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The cost component for fixed manufacturing overhead (plant management, utilities, rent) allocated to one unit based on the costing lot size.']
  freight_in_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The cost component for inbound freight and logistics to bring raw materials and packaging to the plant, allocated to one unit.']
  machine_overhead_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The cost component for machine and equipment overhead (depreciation, maintenance, energy) allocated to one unit based on machine hours.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this standard cost estimate, such as assumptions, special considerations, or reasons for cost changes.']
  packaging_material_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The cost component for packaging materials (primary, secondary, tertiary) used for one unit. Critical for CPG cost structure analysis.']
  previous_standard_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total standard cost from the prior costing period or version. Used for variance analysis and cost trend reporting.']
  raw_material_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The cost component for raw materials consumed in the production of one unit. Key input for COGS and margin analysis.']
  release_date date [note: 'The date when this standard cost estimate was officially released and became active for inventory valuation and COGS calculation.']
  release_status varchar [note: 'The lifecycle status of the standard cost record: draft (under development), released (active for valuation), locked (frozen for audit), or archived (historical).']
  routing_usage varchar [note: 'The routing usage type (e.g., production, costing) that was used as the basis for labor and machine cost calculation in this estimate.']
  total_standard_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total standard cost per unit of the material, summing all cost components (raw material, packaging, labor, overhead, freight). This is the authoritative cost for inventory valuation and COGS.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'The start date from which this standard cost estimate is effective. Used for time-dependent cost valuation and COGS calculation.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'The end date until which this standard cost estimate is effective. Nullable for open-ended validity.']
  valuation_variant varchar [note: 'The valuation variant that defines the costing methodology and cost component structure used for this estimate (e.g., full costing, marginal costing).']
  variable_overhead_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The cost component for variable manufacturing overhead (indirect materials, supplies, variable utilities) allocated to one unit.']

  Note: 'Standard cost master record for finished goods, semi-finished goods, and raw materials used in CPG manufacturing. Stores the cost estimate per SKU/material including cost component split (raw material, packaging, direct labor, machine overhead, fixed overhead, freight-in), costing version, validity period, plant, costing lot size, and release status. The authoritative source for inventory valuation, COGS calculation, and manufacturing variance analysis. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA CO-PC.'
}
